title.zh: 卷二十七下之下 五行志
title.en: Volume 27e: Treatise on the Five Elements 5
title.raw: 卷二十七下之下 五行志 Volume 27e: Treatise on the Five Elements 5

sentences.zh: 隱公三年「二月己巳，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Yin year three: "In the second month, jisi day, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Yin year three: "In the second month, jisi day, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰，言日不言朔，食晦。

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says: it mentions the sun but not the first day, meaning an eclipse at month's end.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says: it mentions the sun but not the first day, meaning an eclipse at month's end.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰，食二日，董仲舒、劉向以為其後戎執天子之使，鄭獲魯隱，滅戴，衛、魯、宋咸殺君。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Tradition says the eclipse lasted two days. Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward the Rong seized the Son of Heaven's envoy; Zheng captured Duke Yin of Lu and destroyed Dai; Wei, Lu, and Song all killed their rulers.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang Tradition says the eclipse lasted two days. Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward the Rong seized the Son of Heaven's envoy; Zheng captured Duke Yin of Lu and destroyed Dai; Wei, Lu, and Song all killed their rulers.

sentences.zh: 左氏劉歆以為正月二日，燕、越之分野也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin, following Zuo, held this was the second day of the first month, in the sectors of Yan and Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin, following Zuo, held this was the second day of the first month, in the sectors of Yan and Yue.

sentences.zh: 凡日所躔而有變，則分野之國失政者受之。

translations.literal: Whenever the sun traverses a sector and an anomaly occurs, states in that sector that lose good governance bear it.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the sun traverses a sector and an anomaly occurs, states in that sector that lose good governance bear it.

sentences.zh: 人君能修政，共御厥罰，則災消而福至；

translations.literal: If a ruler can repair governance and together ward off the punishment, disaster fades and blessing arrives;
translations.idiomatic: If a ruler can repair governance and together ward off the punishment, disaster fades and blessing arrives;

sentences.zh: 不能，則災息而禍生。

translations.literal: if not, the disaster settles and calamity is born.
translations.idiomatic: if not, the disaster settles and calamity is born.

sentences.zh: 故經書災而不記其故，蓋吉凶亡常，隨行而成禍福也。

translations.literal: Thus the Classic records disasters but not their specific causes, for fortune and misfortune are not fixed; blessings and calamities form according to conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Classic records disasters but not their specific causes, for fortune and misfortune are not fixed; blessings and calamities form according to conduct.

sentences.zh: 周衰，天子不班朔，魯曆不正，置閏不得其月，月大小不得其度。

translations.literal: As Zhou declined, the Son of Heaven no longer promulgated the calendar. Lu's reckoning became inaccurate; intercalary months were misplaced, and month lengths lost proper measure.
translations.idiomatic: As Zhou declined, the Son of Heaven no longer promulgated the calendar. Lu's reckoning became inaccurate; intercalary months were misplaced, and month lengths lost proper measure.

sentences.zh: 史記曰食，或言朔而實非朔，或不言朔而實朔，或脫不書朔與日，皆官失之也。

translations.literal: The Records says of eclipses: sometimes it says new moon when it was not new moon, sometimes omits new moon when it was, and sometimes drops both day and new moon; all are failures of officials.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says of eclipses: sometimes it says new moon when it was not new moon, sometimes omits new moon when it was, and sometimes drops both day and new moon; all are failures of officials.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「亡師茲謂不御，厥異日食，其食也既，並食不一處。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Lacking guidance is called failing to control; its anomaly is solar eclipse. When total, and eclipses occur together not in one place.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Lacking guidance is called failing to control; its anomaly is solar eclipse. When total, and eclipses occur together not in one place.

sentences.zh: 誅眾失理，茲謂生叛，厥食既，光散。

translations.literal: If punishments of the masses are mishandled, this is called breeding rebellion; in a total eclipse, light scatters.
translations.idiomatic: If punishments of the masses are mishandled, this is called breeding rebellion; in a total eclipse, light scatters.

sentences.zh: 縱畔茲謂不明，厥食先大雨三日，雨除而寒，寒即食。

translations.literal: If one indulges treachery, this is called lack of clarity; its eclipse is preceded by three days of heavy rain, then cold after rain clears, and then eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: If one indulges treachery, this is called lack of clarity; its eclipse is preceded by three days of heavy rain, then cold after rain clears, and then eclipse.

sentences.zh: 專祿不封，茲謂不安，厥食既，先日出而黑，光反外燭。

translations.literal: If one monopolizes salary and does not enfeoff, this is called instability; in total eclipse, the sun first rises black and its light shines outward in reversal.
translations.idiomatic: If one monopolizes salary and does not enfeoff, this is called instability; in total eclipse, the sun first rises black and its light shines outward in reversal.

sentences.zh: 君臣不通茲謂亡，厥蝕三既。

translations.literal: If ruler and ministers do not communicate, this is called extinction; its eclipse totals three times.
translations.idiomatic: If ruler and ministers do not communicate, this is called extinction; its eclipse totals three times.

sentences.zh: 同姓上侵，茲謂誣君，厥食四方有雲，中央無雲，其日大寒。

translations.literal: If same-surname lines encroach above, this is called deceiving the ruler; in its eclipse there are clouds in four quarters and no cloud at center, and that day is very cold.
translations.idiomatic: If same-surname lines encroach above, this is called deceiving the ruler; in its eclipse there are clouds in four quarters and no cloud at center, and that day is very cold.

sentences.zh: 公欲弱主位，茲謂不知，厥食中白青，四方赤，已食地震。

translations.literal: If dukes seek to weaken the ruler's position, this is called ignorance; in its eclipse the center is white-blue and the four quarters red, and after the eclipse there is earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: If dukes seek to weaken the ruler's position, this is called ignorance; in its eclipse the center is white-blue and the four quarters red, and after the eclipse there is earthquake.

sentences.zh: 諸侯相侵，茲謂不承，厥食三毀三復。

translations.literal: If feudal lords invade one another, this is called failure to uphold; its eclipse has three diminishings and three recoveries.
translations.idiomatic: If feudal lords invade one another, this is called failure to uphold; its eclipse has three diminishings and three recoveries.

sentences.zh: 君疾善，下謀上，茲謂亂，厥食既，先雨雹，殺走獸。

translations.literal: If the ruler hates the good and inferiors plot against superiors, this is called disorder; in total eclipse hail-rain comes first and kills running beasts.
translations.idiomatic: If the ruler hates the good and inferiors plot against superiors, this is called disorder; in total eclipse hail-rain comes first and kills running beasts.

sentences.zh: 弒君獲位茲謂逆，厥食既，先風雨折木，日赤。

translations.literal: If one kills the ruler and seizes the throne, this is called rebellion; in total eclipse wind and rain first break trees and the sun turns red.
translations.idiomatic: If one kills the ruler and seizes the throne, this is called rebellion; in total eclipse wind and rain first break trees and the sun turns red.

sentences.zh: 內臣外鄉茲謂背，厥食食且雨，地中鳴。

translations.literal: If inner ministers lean outward, this is called betrayal; during eclipse there is rain and subterranean rumbling.
translations.idiomatic: If inner ministers lean outward, this is called betrayal; during eclipse there is rain and subterranean rumbling.

sentences.zh: 冢宰專政茲謂因，厥食先大風，食時日居雲中，四方亡雲。

translations.literal: If the chief minister monopolizes government, this is called usurpative continuation; before eclipse comes great wind, and at eclipse the sun sits in cloud while four quarters are cloudless.
translations.idiomatic: If the chief minister monopolizes government, this is called usurpative continuation; before eclipse comes great wind, and at eclipse the sun sits in cloud while four quarters are cloudless.

sentences.zh: 伯正越職，茲謂分威，厥食日中分。

translations.literal: If regional chiefs overstep office, this is called divided authority; in eclipse the sun splits at center.
translations.idiomatic: If regional chiefs overstep office, this is called divided authority; in eclipse the sun splits at center.

sentences.zh: 諸侯爭美於上茲謂泰，厥食日傷月，食半，天營而鳴。

translations.literal: If feudal lords compete for display before the throne, this is called excess; in eclipse the sun injures the moon, half eaten, and heaven rings and sounds.
translations.idiomatic: If feudal lords compete for display before the throne, this is called excess; in eclipse the sun injures the moon, half eaten, and heaven rings and sounds.

sentences.zh: 賦不得茲謂竭，厥食星隨而下。

translations.literal: If taxation cannot be collected, this is called exhaustion; in eclipse stars follow and fall.
translations.idiomatic: If taxation cannot be collected, this is called exhaustion; in eclipse stars follow and fall.

sentences.zh: 受命之臣專征云試，厥食雖侵光猶明，若文王臣獨誅紂矣。

translations.literal: If a commissioned minister personally campaigns, this is called trial; in eclipse though light is invaded it remains bright, like King Wen's minister alone executing Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: If a commissioned minister personally campaigns, this is called trial; in eclipse though light is invaded it remains bright, like King Wen's minister alone executing Zhou.

sentences.zh: 小人順受命者征其君云殺，厥食五色，至大寒隕霜，若紂臣順武王而誅紂矣。

translations.literal: If petty men accept commands compliantly to attack their ruler, this is called killing; its eclipse shows five colors, then severe cold and falling frost, like Zhou's ministers complying with King Wu to execute Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: If petty men accept commands compliantly to attack their ruler, this is called killing; its eclipse shows five colors, then severe cold and falling frost, like Zhou's ministers complying with King Wu to execute Zhou.

sentences.zh: 諸侯更制茲謂叛，厥食三復三食，食已而風，地動。

translations.literal: If feudal lords alter institutions, this is called rebellion; in eclipse there are three recoveries and three eatings, then wind and earth movement after eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: If feudal lords alter institutions, this is called rebellion; in eclipse there are three recoveries and three eatings, then wind and earth movement after eclipse.

sentences.zh: 適讓庶茲謂生欲，厥食日失位，光晻晻，月形見。

translations.literal: If the legitimate yields to the secondary, this is called rising desire; in eclipse the sun loses position, light dim and dark, with moon shape visible.
translations.idiomatic: If the legitimate yields to the secondary, this is called rising desire; in eclipse the sun loses position, light dim and dark, with moon shape visible.

sentences.zh: 酒亡節茲謂荒，厥蝕乍青乍黑乍赤，明日大雨，發霧而寒。」

translations.literal: If drinking lacks restraint, this is called dissipation; its eclipse turns blue, then black, then red; next day heavy rain, rising mist, and cold."
translations.idiomatic: If drinking lacks restraint, this is called dissipation; its eclipse turns blue, then black, then red; next day heavy rain, rising mist, and cold."

sentences.zh: 凡食二十占，其形二十有四，改之輒除；

translations.literal: In all there are twenty eclipse divinations and twenty-four forms; if one reforms, they are removed at once;
translations.idiomatic: In all there are twenty eclipse divinations and twenty-four forms; if one reforms, they are removed at once;

sentences.zh: 不改三年，三年不改六年，六年不改九年。

translations.literal: if not in three years, then six; if not in six, then nine.
translations.idiomatic: if not in three years, then six; if not in six, then nine.

sentences.zh: 推隱三年之食，貫中央，上下竟而黑，臣弒從中成之形也。

translations.literal: For the eclipse in Yin year three, the line pierced the center, black through top and bottom, form of ministers completing regicide from the center.
translations.idiomatic: For the eclipse in Yin year three, the line pierced the center, black through top and bottom, form of ministers completing regicide from the center.

sentences.zh: 後衛州吁弒君而立。

translations.literal: Afterward Wei Zhouyu regicide-killed his ruler and took the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Wei Zhouyu regicide-killed his ruler and took the throne.

sentences.zh: 桓公三年「七月壬辰朔，日有食之，既」。

translations.literal: In Duke Huan year three: "On renchen, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Huan year three: "On renchen, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為前事已大，後事將至者又大，則既。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that when prior events are already great and later events to come are greater, the eclipse is total.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that when prior events are already great and later events to come are greater, the eclipse is total.

sentences.zh: 先是魯、宋弒君，魯又成宋亂，易許田，亡事天子之心；

translations.literal: Before this Lu and Song had regicide-killed rulers; Lu further worsened Song's disorder, exchanged Xu fields, and lacked heart to serve the Son of Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Before this Lu and Song had regicide-killed rulers; Lu further worsened Song's disorder, exchanged Xu fields, and lacked heart to serve the Son of Heaven;

sentences.zh: 楚僭稱王。

translations.literal: Chu presumptuously claimed kingship.
translations.idiomatic: Chu presumptuously claimed kingship.

sentences.zh: 後鄭岠王師，射桓王，又二君相篡。

translations.literal: Afterward Zheng resisted the royal army and shot King Huan, and two rulers in turn usurped one another.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Zheng resisted the royal army and shot King Huan, and two rulers in turn usurped one another.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為六月，趙與晉分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the sixth month, sector of Zhao and Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the sixth month, sector of Zhao and Jin.

sentences.zh: 先是，晉曲沃伯再弒晉侯，是歲晉大亂，滅其宗國。

translations.literal: Before this, the Earl of Quwo in Jin had twice regicide-killed Jin marquises; that year Jin was in great chaos and extinguished its own ancestral state.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, the Earl of Quwo in Jin had twice regicide-killed Jin marquises; that year Jin was in great chaos and extinguished its own ancestral state.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳以為桓三年日食貫中央，上下竟而黃，臣弒而不卒之形也。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition held the eclipse in Huan year three pierced the center, black top to bottom with yellow hue, form of ministers killing ruler without completing it.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition held the eclipse in Huan year three pierced the center, black top to bottom with yellow hue, form of ministers killing ruler without completing it.

sentences.zh: 後楚嚴稱王，兼地千里。

translations.literal: Afterward Chu solemnly styled itself king and annexed a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Chu solemnly styled itself king and annexed a thousand li.

sentences.zh: 十七年「十月朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year seventeen: "On first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen: "On first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰，言朔不言日，食二日也。

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says: mentioning first day but not day means an eclipse lasting two days.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says: mentioning first day but not day means an eclipse lasting two days.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時衛侯朔有罪出奔齊，天子更立衛君。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that then Marquis Shuo of Wei, guilty, fled to Qi, and the Son of Heaven installed another ruler of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that then Marquis Shuo of Wei, guilty, fled to Qi, and the Son of Heaven installed another ruler of Wei.

sentences.zh: 朔藉助五國，舉兵伐之而自立，王命遂壞。

translations.literal: Shuo borrowed aid of five states, raised troops to attack, and reinstalled himself, so royal commands were broken.
translations.idiomatic: Shuo borrowed aid of five states, raised troops to attack, and reinstalled himself, so royal commands were broken.

sentences.zh: 魯夫人淫失於齊，卒殺威公。

translations.literal: The lady of Lu became licentious with Qi and eventually killed Duke Huan.
translations.idiomatic: The lady of Lu became licentious with Qi and eventually killed Duke Huan.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為言朔不言日，惡魯桓且有夫人之禍，將不終日也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that mentioning first day but not day condemned Lu's Duke Huan and signaled the lady's coming disaster, that he would not complete his days.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that mentioning first day but not day condemned Lu's Duke Huan and signaled the lady's coming disaster, that he would not complete his days.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為楚、鄭分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this belonged to the sectors of Chu and Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this belonged to the sectors of Chu and Zheng.

sentences.zh: 嚴公十八年「三月，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In the third month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In the third month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰，不言日，不言朔，夜食。

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says: not mentioning day and not mentioning first day means a night eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says: not mentioning day and not mentioning first day means a night eclipse.

sentences.zh: 史推合朔在夜，明旦日食而出，出而解，是為夜食。

translations.literal: Astronomical reconstruction places conjunction at night: at dawn the sun emerged eclipsed, then cleared after rising; this is called night eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: Astronomical reconstruction places conjunction at night: at dawn the sun emerged eclipsed, then cleared after rising; this is called night eclipse.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為夜食者，陰因日明之衰而奪其光，象周天子不明，齊桓將奪其威，專會諸侯而行伯道。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that in night eclipse yin takes advantage of waning solar brightness to seize its light, symbolizing the Zhou Son of Heaven lacking clarity while Duke Huan of Qi would seize his authority, monopolizing feudal meetings and practicing hegemony.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in night eclipse yin takes advantage of waning solar brightness to seize its light, symbolizing the Zhou Son of Heaven lacking clarity while Duke Huan of Qi would seize his authority, monopolizing feudal meetings and practicing hegemony.

sentences.zh: 其後遂九合諸侯，天子使世子會之，此其效也。

translations.literal: Afterward he indeed convened feudal lords nine times, and the Son of Heaven sent the heir apparent to attend him; this was its fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he indeed convened feudal lords nine times, and the Son of Heaven sent the heir apparent to attend him; this was its fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰食晦。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Tradition says eclipse at month's end.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang Tradition says eclipse at month's end.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在東壁，魯象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Eastern Wall, symbol of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Eastern Wall, symbol of Lu.

sentences.zh: 後公子慶父、叔牙果通於夫人以劫公。

translations.literal: Afterward Prince Qingfu and Shuya indeed colluded with the lady to coerce the duke.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Prince Qingfu and Shuya indeed colluded with the lady to coerce the duke.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為晦魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that month-end meant sectors of Lu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that month-end meant sectors of Lu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 二十五年「六月辛未朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-five: "On xinwei, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-five: "On xinwei, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在畢，主邊兵夷狄象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Bi, governing frontier armies and symbols of border tribes.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Bi, governing frontier armies and symbols of border tribes.

sentences.zh: 後狄滅邢、衛。

translations.literal: Afterward the Di destroyed Xing and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Di destroyed Xing and Wei.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為五月二日魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 二十六年「十二月癸亥朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-six: "On guihai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-six: "On guihai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在心，心為明堂，文武之道廢，中國不絕若悋之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart; Heart is the Bright Hall. It signaled abandonment of civil and martial way, with central states not yet cut off but on edge of collapse.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart; Heart is the Bright Hall. It signaled abandonment of civil and martial way, with central states not yet cut off but on edge of collapse.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時戎侵曹，魯夫人淫於慶父、叔牙，將以弒君，故比年再蝕以見戒。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that then Rong invaded Cao, and the lady of Lu was licentious with Qingfu and Shuya and would commit regicide; therefore eclipses came in successive years as warning.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that then Rong invaded Cao, and the lady of Lu was licentious with Qingfu and Shuya and would commit regicide; therefore eclipses came in successive years as warning.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為十月二日楚、鄭分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was second day of tenth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was second day of tenth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.

sentences.zh: 三十年「九月庚午朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year thirty: "On gengwu, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year thirty: "On gengwu, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後魯二君弒，夫人誅，兩弟死，狄滅邢，徐取舒，晉殺世子，楚滅弦。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward two rulers of Lu were slain, the lady was executed, two younger brothers died, Di destroyed Xing, Xu took Shu, Jin killed its heir, and Chu destroyed Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward two rulers of Lu were slain, the lady was executed, two younger brothers died, Di destroyed Xing, Xu took Shu, Jin killed its heir, and Chu destroyed Xian.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為八月秦、周分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in the eighth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 僖公五年「九月戊申朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year five: "On wushen, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year five: "On wushen, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是齊桓行伯，江、黃自至，南服彊楚。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Huan of Qi practiced hegemony, and Jiang and Huang came of themselves; strong Chu in the south was checked.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Huan of Qi practiced hegemony, and Jiang and Huang came of themselves; strong Chu in the south was checked.

sentences.zh: 其後不內自正，而外執陳大夫，則陳、楚不附，鄭伯逃盟，諸侯將不從桓政，故天見戒。

translations.literal: Afterward he failed to correct himself within, and instead seized a Chen grandee abroad; Chen and Chu then no longer adhered, and the earl of Zheng fled the covenant. Feudal lords would no longer follow Huan's policy, so Heaven gave warning.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he failed to correct himself within, and instead seized a Chen grandee abroad; Chen and Chu then no longer adhered, and the earl of Zheng fled the covenant. Feudal lords would no longer follow Huan's policy, so Heaven gave warning.

sentences.zh: 其後晉滅虢，楚國許，諸侯伐鄭，晉弒二君，狄滅溫，楚伐黃，桓不能救。

translations.literal: Afterward Jin destroyed Guo, Chu reduced Xu, feudal lords attacked Zheng, Jin regicide-killed two rulers, Di destroyed Wen, and Chu attacked Huang; Huan could not rescue them.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Jin destroyed Guo, Chu reduced Xu, feudal lords attacked Zheng, Jin regicide-killed two rulers, Di destroyed Wen, and Chu attacked Huang; Huan could not rescue them.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為七月秦、晉分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Qin and Jin in seventh month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Qin and Jin in seventh month.

sentences.zh: 十二年「三月庚午朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twelve: "On gengwu, first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twelve: "On gengwu, first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為是時楚滅黃，狄侵衛、鄭，莒滅杞。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that then Chu destroyed Huang, Di invaded Wei and Zheng, and Ju destroyed Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that then Chu destroyed Huang, Di invaded Wei and Zheng, and Ju destroyed Qi.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為三月齊、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qi and Wei in third month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qi and Wei in third month.

sentences.zh: 十五年「五月，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "In fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "In fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為象晉文公將行伯道，後遂伐衛，執曹伯，敗楚城濮，再會諸侯，召天王而朝之，此其效也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this symbolized Duke Wen of Jin about to practice hegemony: he later attacked Wei, captured the earl of Cao, defeated Chu at Chengpu, convened feudal lords twice, and summoned the Son of Heaven to receive homage; this was its fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this symbolized Duke Wen of Jin about to practice hegemony: he later attacked Wei, captured the earl of Cao, defeated Chu at Chengpu, convened feudal lords twice, and summoned the Son of Heaven to receive homage; this was its fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 日食者臣之惡也，夜食者掩其罪也，以為上亡明王，桓、文能行伯道，攘夷狄，安中國，雖不正猶可，蓋春秋實與而文不與之義也。

translations.literal: Solar eclipse marks ministerial evil; night eclipse conceals the crime. Liu held that with no bright king above, if Huan and Wen could practice hegemony, repel tribes, and stabilize the central states, though not fully orthodox it was still acceptable: the Spring and Autumn principle is to approve in substance while withholding praise in wording.
translations.idiomatic: Solar eclipse marks ministerial evil; night eclipse conceals the crime. Liu held that with no bright king above, if Huan and Wen could practice hegemony, repel tribes, and stabilize the central states, though not fully orthodox it was still acceptable: the Spring and Autumn principle is to approve in substance while withholding praise in wording.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為後秦獲晉侯，齊滅項，楚敗徐于婁林。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that afterward Qin captured the marquis of Jin, Qi destroyed Xiang, and Chu defeated Xu at Loulin.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that afterward Qin captured the marquis of Jin, Qi destroyed Xiang, and Chu defeated Xu at Loulin.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為二月朔齊、越分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was first day of second month, sectors of Qi and Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was first day of second month, sectors of Qi and Yue.

sentences.zh: 十五年「六月辛丑朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "On xinchou, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "On xinchou, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後宋、齊、莒、晉、鄭八年之間五君殺死，夷滅舒蓼。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward, within eight years among Song, Qi, Ju, Jin, and Zheng, five rulers were killed, and Shu and Liao were exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward, within eight years among Song, Qi, Ju, Jin, and Zheng, five rulers were killed, and Shu and Liao were exterminated.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為四月二日魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fourth month, sectors of Lu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fourth month, sectors of Lu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 宣公八年「七月甲子，日有食之，既」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xuan year eight: "On jiazi of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year eight: "On jiazi of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是楚商臣弒父而立，至于嚴王遂彊。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Shangchen of Chu killed his father and took the throne; by King Zhuang of Chu, power had grown strong.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Shangchen of Chu killed his father and took the throne; by King Zhuang of Chu, power had grown strong.

sentences.zh: 諸夏大國唯有齊、晉，齊、晉新有篡弒之禍，內皆未安，故楚乘弱橫行，八年之間六侵伐而一滅國；

translations.literal: Among the Xia states only Qi and Jin were major powers, yet both had recently suffered usurpation and regicide and remained unstable within, so Chu exploited their weakness and ran rampant, launching six invasions and destroying one state within eight years;
translations.idiomatic: Among the Xia states only Qi and Jin were major powers, yet both had recently suffered usurpation and regicide and remained unstable within, so Chu exploited their weakness and ran rampant, launching six invasions and destroying one state within eight years;

sentences.zh: 伐陸渾戎，觀兵周室；

translations.literal: it attacked the Luhun Rong and reviewed troops before Zhou's royal house;
translations.idiomatic: it attacked the Luhun Rong and reviewed troops before Zhou's royal house;

sentences.zh: 後又入鄭，鄭伯肉袒謝罪；

translations.literal: later it entered Zheng, and the earl of Zheng stripped his upper body to apologize;
translations.idiomatic: later it entered Zheng, and the earl of Zheng stripped his upper body to apologize;

sentences.zh: 北敗晉師于邲，流血色水；

translations.literal: to the north it defeated Jin at Bi, with blood reddening the waters;
translations.idiomatic: to the north it defeated Jin at Bi, with blood reddening the waters;

sentences.zh: 圍宋九月，析骸而炊之。

translations.literal: it besieged Song for nine months, until they split bones for fuel and cooked them.
translations.idiomatic: it besieged Song for nine months, until they split bones for fuel and cooked them.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為十月二日楚、鄭分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of tenth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of tenth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.

sentences.zh: 十年「四月丙辰，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year ten: "On bingchen day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year ten: "On bingchen day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後陳夏徵舒弒其君，楚滅蕭，晉滅二國，王札子殺召伯、毛伯。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Xia Zhengshu of Chen killed his ruler, Chu destroyed Xiao, Jin destroyed two states, and Prince Zha killed Duke Zhao and Duke Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Xia Zhengshu of Chen killed his ruler, Chu destroyed Xiao, Jin destroyed two states, and Prince Zha killed Duke Zhao and Duke Mao.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為二月魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in second month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in second month.

sentences.zh: 十七年「六月癸卯，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year seventeen: "On guimao day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen: "On guimao day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後邾支解鄫子，晉敗王師于貿戎，敗齊于鞍。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Zhu dismembered the ruler of Zeng, Jin defeated the royal army at Mao-Rong, and defeated Qi at An.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Zhu dismembered the ruler of Zeng, Jin defeated the royal army at Mao-Rong, and defeated Qi at An.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為三月晦朓魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was month-end conjunction in third month, sectors of Lu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was month-end conjunction in third month, sectors of Lu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 成公十六年「六月丙寅朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On bingyin, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On bingyin, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後晉敗楚、鄭于鄢陵，執魯侯。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Jin defeated Chu and Zheng at Yanling and captured the ruler of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Jin defeated Chu and Zheng at Yanling and captured the ruler of Lu.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為四月二日魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fourth month, sectors of Lu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fourth month, sectors of Lu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 十七年「十二月丁巳朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year seventeen: "On dingsi, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen: "On dingsi, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後楚滅舒庸，晉弒其君，宋魚石因楚奪君邑，莒滅鄫，齊滅萊，鄭伯弒死。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Chu destroyed Shuyong, Jin regicide-killed its ruler, Song's Yu Shi used Chu to seize rulerly towns, Ju destroyed Zeng, Qi destroyed Lai, and the earl of Zheng died by regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Chu destroyed Shuyong, Jin regicide-killed its ruler, Song's Yu Shi used Chu to seize rulerly towns, Ju destroyed Zeng, Qi destroyed Lai, and the earl of Zheng died by regicide.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為九月周、楚分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Zhou and Chu in ninth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Zhou and Chu in ninth month.

sentences.zh: 襄公十四年「二月乙未朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang year fourteen: "On yiwei, first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year fourteen: "On yiwei, first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後衛大夫孫、甯共逐獻公，立孫剽。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Sun and Ning, grandees of Wei, jointly expelled Duke Xian and installed Sun Piao.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Sun and Ning, grandees of Wei, jointly expelled Duke Xian and installed Sun Piao.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為前年十二月二日宋、燕分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of the previous year's twelfth month, sectors of Song and Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of the previous year's twelfth month, sectors of Song and Yan.

sentences.zh: 十五年「八月丁巳，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "On dingsi of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "On dingsi of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是晉為雞澤之會，諸侯盟，又大夫盟，後為溴梁之會，諸侯在而大夫獨相與盟，君若綴斿，不得舉手。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Jin held the Jize assembly, where feudal lords covenanted and then grandees covenanted; later at the Chouliang assembly, feudal lords were present while grandees covenanted by themselves, rulers like hanging tassels unable to raise a hand.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Jin held the Jize assembly, where feudal lords covenanted and then grandees covenanted; later at the Chouliang assembly, feudal lords were present while grandees covenanted by themselves, rulers like hanging tassels unable to raise a hand.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為五月二日魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 二十年「十月丙辰朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty: "On bingchen, first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty: "On bingchen, first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為陳慶虎、慶寅蔽君之明，邾庶其有叛心，後庶其以漆、閭丘來奔，陳殺二慶。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that in Chen, Qing Hu and Qing Yin obscured rulerly clarity, and in Zhu, Shuqi harbored rebellion; later Shuqi fled with Qi and Lüqiu, and Chen killed the two Qings.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that in Chen, Qing Hu and Qing Yin obscured rulerly clarity, and in Zhu, Shuqi harbored rebellion; later Shuqi fled with Qi and Lüqiu, and Chen killed the two Qings.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為八月秦、周分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in eighth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in eighth month.

sentences.zh: 二十一年「九月庚戌朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-one: "On gengxu, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-one: "On gengxu, first day of ninth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為晉欒盈將犯君，後入于曲沃。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that Luan Ying of Jin would offend the ruler; later he entered Quwo.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that Luan Ying of Jin would offend the ruler; later he entered Quwo.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為七月秦、晉分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Jin in seventh month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Jin in seventh month.

sentences.zh: 「十月庚辰朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: "On gengchen, first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: "On gengchen, first day of tenth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在軫、角，楚大國象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Zhen and Jiao, symbols of the great state Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Zhen and Jiao, symbols of the great state Chu.

sentences.zh: 後楚屈氏譖殺公子追舒，齊慶封脅君亂國。

translations.literal: Afterward the Qu clan of Chu slander-killed Prince Zhuishu, and Qing Feng of Qi coerced his ruler and disordered the state.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Qu clan of Chu slander-killed Prince Zhuishu, and Qing Feng of Qi coerced his ruler and disordered the state.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為八月秦、周分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in eighth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Qin and Zhou in eighth month.

sentences.zh: 二十三年「二月癸酉朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-three: "On guiyou, first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-three: "On guiyou, first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為後衛侯入陳儀，甯喜弒其君剽。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that afterward the marquis of Wei entered with Chen Yi, and Ning Xi regicide-killed his ruler Piao.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that afterward the marquis of Wei entered with Chen Yi, and Ning Xi regicide-killed his ruler Piao.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為前年十二月二日宋、燕分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of the previous twelfth month, sectors of Song and Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of the previous twelfth month, sectors of Song and Yan.

sentences.zh: 二十四年「七月甲子朔，日有食之，既」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-four: "On jiazi, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-four: "On jiazi, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed, total."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為五月魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Zhao in fifth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Zhao in fifth month.

sentences.zh: 「八月癸巳朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: "On guisi, first day of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: "On guisi, first day of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為比食又既，象陽將絕，夷狄主上國之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that repeated eclipses, again including totality, symbolized yang nearing extinction and border peoples about to dominate the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that repeated eclipses, again including totality, symbolized yang nearing extinction and border peoples about to dominate the central states.

sentences.zh: 後六君弒，楚子果從諸侯伐鄭，滅舒鳩，魯往朝之，卒主中國，伐吳討慶封。

translations.literal: Afterward six rulers were regicide-killed; the ruler of Chu indeed joined feudal lords to attack Zheng, destroyed Shujiu, was visited by Lu in court homage, ultimately led the central states, attacked Wu, and punished Qing Feng.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward six rulers were regicide-killed; the ruler of Chu indeed joined feudal lords to attack Zheng, destroyed Shujiu, was visited by Lu in court homage, ultimately led the central states, attacked Wu, and punished Qing Feng.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為六月晉、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Jin and Zhao in sixth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Jin and Zhao in sixth month.

sentences.zh: 二十七年「十二月乙亥朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-seven: "On yihai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-seven: "On yihai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為禮義將大滅絕之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held this was an image that rites and righteousness were nearing complete extinction.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held this was an image that rites and righteousness were nearing complete extinction.

sentences.zh: 時吳子好勇，使刑人守門；

translations.literal: At that time the ruler of Wu delighted in valor and put penal men as gate guards;
translations.idiomatic: At that time the ruler of Wu delighted in valor and put penal men as gate guards;

sentences.zh: 蔡侯通於世子之妻；

translations.literal: the marquis of Cai had relations with his heir's wife;
translations.idiomatic: the marquis of Cai had relations with his heir's wife;

sentences.zh: 莒不早立嗣。

translations.literal: and Ju failed to establish succession early.
translations.idiomatic: and Ju failed to establish succession early.

sentences.zh: 後閽戕吳子，蔡世子般弒其父，莒人亦弒君而庶子爭。

translations.literal: Afterward gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, the heir Ban of Cai regicide-killed his father, and in Ju too people killed the ruler and secondary sons contended.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, the heir Ban of Cai regicide-killed his father, and in Ju too people killed the ruler and secondary sons contended.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為自二十年至此歲，八年間日食七作，禍亂將重起，故天仍見戒也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that from year twenty to this year, seven eclipses occurred in eight years: disasters and disorder were about to rise heavily again, so Heaven repeatedly warned.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that from year twenty to this year, seven eclipses occurred in eight years: disasters and disorder were about to rise heavily again, so Heaven repeatedly warned.

sentences.zh: 後齊崔杼弒君，宋殺世子，北燕伯出奔，鄭大夫自外入而篡位，指略如董仲舒。

translations.literal: Afterward Cui Zhu of Qi regicide-killed his ruler, Song killed its heir, the Earl of Northern Yan fled, and a Zheng grandee entered from abroad and usurped the throne; the main points match Dong Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Cui Zhu of Qi regicide-killed his ruler, Song killed its heir, the Earl of Northern Yan fled, and a Zheng grandee entered from abroad and usurped the throne; the main points match Dong Zhongshu.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為九月周、楚分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Zhou and Chu in ninth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Zhou and Chu in ninth month.

sentences.zh: 昭公七年「四月甲辰朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year seven: "On jiachen, first day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year seven: "On jiachen, first day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是楚靈王弒君而立，會諸侯，執徐子，滅賴，後陳公子招殺世子，楚因而滅之，又滅蔡，後靈王亦弒死。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this King Ling of Chu had regicide-killed and taken the throne, convened feudal lords, seized the ruler of Xu, and destroyed Lai; afterward Prince Zhao of Chen killed the heir, and Chu then destroyed Chen, then Cai; later King Ling himself died by regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this King Ling of Chu had regicide-killed and taken the throne, convened feudal lords, seized the ruler of Xu, and destroyed Lai; afterward Prince Zhao of Chen killed the heir, and Chu then destroyed Chen, then Cai; later King Ling himself died by regicide.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為二月魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in second month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in second month.

sentences.zh: 傳曰晉侯問於士文伯曰：「誰將當日食？」

translations.literal: The Tradition says the marquis of Jin asked Shi Wenbo: "Who will bear this eclipse?"
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says the marquis of Jin asked Shi Wenbo: "Who will bear this eclipse?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「魯、衛惡之，衛大魯小。」

translations.literal: He replied: "Lu and Wei will suffer it; Wei greater, Lu lesser."
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Lu and Wei will suffer it; Wei greater, Lu lesser."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「何故？」

translations.literal: The duke said: "Why?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke said: "Why?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「去衛地，如魯地，於是有災，其衛君乎？

translations.literal: He replied: "From Wei's territory to Lu's territory, thus there is disaster. Will it be the ruler of Wei?
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "From Wei's territory to Lu's territory, thus there is disaster. Will it be the ruler of Wei?

sentences.zh: 魯將上卿。」

translations.literal: Lu will lose its highest minister."
translations.idiomatic: Lu will lose its highest minister."

sentences.zh: 是歲，八月衛襄公卒，十一月魯季孫宿卒。

translations.literal: That year, in eighth month Duke Xiang of Wei died; in eleventh month Jisun Su of Lu died.
translations.idiomatic: That year, in eighth month Duke Xiang of Wei died; in eleventh month Jisun Su of Lu died.

sentences.zh: 晉侯謂士文伯曰：「吾所問日食從矣，可常乎？」

translations.literal: The marquis of Jin said to Shi Wenbo: "What I asked about eclipse was fulfilled. Can this be constant?"
translations.idiomatic: The marquis of Jin said to Shi Wenbo: "What I asked about eclipse was fulfilled. Can this be constant?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「不可。

translations.literal: He replied: "It cannot.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "It cannot.

sentences.zh: 六物不同，民心不壹，事序不類，官職不則，同始異終，胡可常也？

translations.literal: The six things differ, people's hearts are not one, events and sequences are not alike, offices and duties are not regular; same beginning yet different end, how could it be constant?
translations.idiomatic: The six things differ, people's hearts are not one, events and sequences are not alike, offices and duties are not regular; same beginning yet different end, how could it be constant?

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：『或宴宴居息，或盡悴事國。』

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Some dwell at ease in banquet-rest, others are worn out serving the state."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Some dwell at ease in banquet-rest, others are worn out serving the state."

sentences.zh: 其異終也如是。」

translations.literal: Their endings differ just so."
translations.idiomatic: Their endings differ just so."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「何謂六物？」

translations.literal: The duke said: "What are the six things?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke said: "What are the six things?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「

translations.literal: He replied: "
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "

sentences.zh: 歲、時、日、月、星、辰是謂。」

translations.literal: Year, season, day, month, stars, and constellations are these."
translations.idiomatic: Year, season, day, month, stars, and constellations are these."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「何謂辰？」

translations.literal: The duke said: "What is meant by constellation?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke said: "What is meant by constellation?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「日月之會是謂。」

translations.literal: He replied: "It means where sun and moon meet."
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "It means where sun and moon meet."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「詩所謂『此日而食，于何不臧』，何也？」

translations.literal: The duke said: "The poem says, "On this day the sun was eclipsed; why was it not good?" What does this mean?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke said: "The poem says, "On this day the sun was eclipsed; why was it not good?" What does this mean?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「不善政之謂也。

translations.literal: He replied: "It means bad governance.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "It means bad governance.

sentences.zh: 國無政，不用善，則自取適于日月之災。

translations.literal: If a state lacks governance and does not use the good, it draws onto itself the disasters of sun and moon.
translations.idiomatic: If a state lacks governance and does not use the good, it draws onto itself the disasters of sun and moon.

sentences.zh: 故政不可不慎也，務三而已：一曰擇人，二曰因民，三曰從時。」

translations.literal: Therefore governance cannot but be cautious, and concerns only three tasks: first choosing people, second adapting to the people, third following the season."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore governance cannot but be cautious, and concerns only three tasks: first choosing people, second adapting to the people, third following the season."

sentences.zh: 此推日食之占循變復之要也。

translations.literal: This infers from eclipse divination the key to cyclical change and return.
translations.idiomatic: This infers from eclipse divination the key to cyclical change and return.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「縣象著明，莫大於日月。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Of hanging signs made manifest, nothing is greater than sun and moon."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "Of hanging signs made manifest, nothing is greater than sun and moon."

sentences.zh: 是故聖人重之，載于三經。

translations.literal: Therefore sages gave them great weight and recorded them in the three classics.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore sages gave them great weight and recorded them in the three classics.

sentences.zh: 於易在豐之震曰：「豐其沛，日中見昧，折其右肱，亡咎。」

translations.literal: In the Changes under Feng changing to Zhen it says: "Abundance under heavy cover; at noon one sees dimness; his right arm is broken; no blame."
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes under Feng changing to Zhen it says: "Abundance under heavy cover; at noon one sees dimness; his right arm is broken; no blame."

sentences.zh: 於詩十月之交，則著卿士、司徒，下至趣馬、師氏，咸非其材。

translations.literal: In the Odes, "Crossing of the Tenth Month" then names from high ministers and Minister of Works down to horse keepers and masters, all unfit for their talents.
translations.idiomatic: In the Odes, "Crossing of the Tenth Month" then names from high ministers and Minister of Works down to horse keepers and masters, all unfit for their talents.

sentences.zh: 同於右肱之所折，協於三務之所擇，明小人乘君子，陰侵陽之原也。

translations.literal: This matches the broken right arm and accords with choosing by the three duties, showing petty men riding over gentlemen and yin invading yang at its root.
translations.idiomatic: This matches the broken right arm and accords with choosing by the three duties, showing petty men riding over gentlemen and yin invading yang at its root.

sentences.zh: 十五年「六月丁巳朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "On dingsi, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "On dingsi, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為三月魯、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in third month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Wei in third month.

sentences.zh: 十七年「六月甲戌朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year seventeen: "On jiaxu, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen: "On jiaxu, first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為時宿在畢，晉國象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge then was Bi, symbol of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge then was Bi, symbol of Jin.

sentences.zh: 晉厲公誅四大夫，失眾心，以弒死。

translations.literal: Duke Li of Jin executed four great officers, lost the people's support, and died by regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Li of Jin executed four great officers, lost the people's support, and died by regicide.

sentences.zh: 後莫敢復責大夫，六卿遂相與比周，專晉國，君還事之。

translations.literal: Afterward none dared again restrain grandees; the six ministers then allied and monopolized Jin, and rulers in turn served them.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward none dared again restrain grandees; the six ministers then allied and monopolized Jin, and rulers in turn served them.

sentences.zh: 日比再食，其事在春秋後，故不載於經。

translations.literal: The sun was eclipsed repeatedly; since this occurred after the Spring and Autumn period, it was not recorded in the Classic.
translations.idiomatic: The sun was eclipsed repeatedly; since this occurred after the Spring and Autumn period, it was not recorded in the Classic.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Lu and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳平子曰：「唯正月朔，慝未作，日有食之，於是乎天子不舉，伐鼓於社，諸侯用幣於社，伐鼓於朝，禮也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says Pingzi said: "Only on first day of first month, before hidden evils arise, if the sun is eclipsed, then the Son of Heaven suspends affairs and beats drums at the altar; feudal lords use silk offerings at the altar and beat drums at court. This is ritual.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says Pingzi said: "Only on first day of first month, before hidden evils arise, if the sun is eclipsed, then the Son of Heaven suspends affairs and beats drums at the altar; feudal lords use silk offerings at the altar and beat drums at court. This is ritual.

sentences.zh: 其餘則否。」

translations.literal: At other times, no."
translations.idiomatic: At other times, no."

sentences.zh: 太史曰：「在此月也，日過分而未至，三辰有災，百官降物，君不舉，避移時，樂奏鼓，祝用幣，史用辭，嗇夫馳，庶人走，此月朔之謂也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: "In this month, the sun has passed division but not yet arrived; if the three celestial bodies suffer anomaly, the hundred offices lower insignia, the ruler suspends affairs, withdraws for a time-shift, music and drums are played, invocators use offerings, scribes use formulas, tally officers run, commoners run. This is what first-day of this month means.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian said: "In this month, the sun has passed division but not yet arrived; if the three celestial bodies suffer anomaly, the hundred offices lower insignia, the ruler suspends affairs, withdraws for a time-shift, music and drums are played, invocators use offerings, scribes use formulas, tally officers run, commoners run. This is what first-day of this month means.

sentences.zh: 當夏四月，是謂孟夏。」

translations.literal: By Xia reckoning this is fourth month, called early summer."
translations.idiomatic: By Xia reckoning this is fourth month, called early summer."

sentences.zh: 說曰：正月謂周六月，夏四月，正陽純乾之月也。

translations.literal: The explanation says first month means Zhou sixth month, Xia fourth month, month of pure yang and pure Qian.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says first month means Zhou sixth month, Xia fourth month, month of pure yang and pure Qian.

sentences.zh: 慝謂陰爻也，冬至陽爻起初，故曰復。

translations.literal: Hidden evil means yin lines; at winter solstice the first yang line arises, therefore it is called Return.
translations.idiomatic: Hidden evil means yin lines; at winter solstice the first yang line arises, therefore it is called Return.

sentences.zh: 至建巳之月為純乾，亡陰爻，而陰侵陽，為災重，故伐鼓用幣，責陰之禮。

translations.literal: By the month jisi it becomes pure Qian with no yin lines; yet if yin invades yang, the disaster is grave, therefore drums and silk offerings are used as ritual of reproaching yin.
translations.idiomatic: By the month jisi it becomes pure Qian with no yin lines; yet if yin invades yang, the disaster is grave, therefore drums and silk offerings are used as ritual of reproaching yin.

sentences.zh: 降物，素服也。

translations.literal: Lowering insignia means plain mourning dress.
translations.idiomatic: Lowering insignia means plain mourning dress.

sentences.zh: 不舉，去樂也。

translations.literal: Not undertaking means removing music.
translations.idiomatic: Not undertaking means removing music.

sentences.zh: 避移時，避正堂，須時移災復也。

translations.literal: Withdrawing for a time-shift means leaving the main hall, awaiting the time for disaster to pass and return.
translations.idiomatic: Withdrawing for a time-shift means leaving the main hall, awaiting the time for disaster to pass and return.

sentences.zh: 嗇夫，掌幣吏。

translations.literal: The sefu was the official in charge of tally silk offerings.
translations.idiomatic: The sefu was the official in charge of tally silk offerings.

sentences.zh: 庶人，其徒役也。

translations.literal: Commoners means the laboring attendants.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners means the laboring attendants.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為六月二日魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of sixth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of sixth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 二十一年「七月壬午朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-one: "On renwu, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-one: "On renwu, first day of seventh month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為周景王老，劉子、單子專權，蔡侯朱驕，君臣不說之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that King Jing of Zhou was old, while Liuzi and Danzi monopolized power, and Marquis Zhu of Cai was arrogant, image of ruler and ministers not in accord.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that King Jing of Zhou was old, while Liuzi and Danzi monopolized power, and Marquis Zhu of Cai was arrogant, image of ruler and ministers not in accord.

sentences.zh: 後蔡侯朱果出奔，劉子、單子立王猛。

translations.literal: Afterward Marquis Zhu of Cai indeed fled, and Liuzi and Danzi installed Prince Meng.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Marquis Zhu of Cai indeed fled, and Liuzi and Danzi installed Prince Meng.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為五月二日魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of fifth month, sectors of Lu and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 二十二年「十二月癸酉朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-two: "On guiyou, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-two: "On guiyou, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在心，天子之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart, symbol of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart, symbol of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 後尹氏立王子朝，天王居于狄泉。

translations.literal: Afterward the Yin clan installed Prince Chao, and the Son of Heaven resided at Diquan.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Yin clan installed Prince Chao, and the Son of Heaven resided at Diquan.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為十月楚、鄭分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Chu and Zheng in tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Chu and Zheng in tenth month.

sentences.zh: 二十四年「五月乙未朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-four: "On yiwei, first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-four: "On yiwei, first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在胃，魯象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Stomach, symbol of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Stomach, symbol of Lu.

sentences.zh: 後昭公為季氏所逐。

translations.literal: Afterward Duke Zhao was expelled by the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Duke Zhao was expelled by the Ji clan.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為自十五年至此歲，十年間天戒七見，人君猶不寤。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that from year fifteen to this year, in ten years Heaven gave warning seven times, yet rulers still did not awaken.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that from year fifteen to this year, in ten years Heaven gave warning seven times, yet rulers still did not awaken.

sentences.zh: 後楚殺戎蠻子，晉滅陸渾戎，盜殺衛侯兄，蔡、莒之君出奔，吳滅巢，公子光殺王僚，宋三臣以邑叛其君。

translations.literal: Afterward Chu killed the ruler of Rongmanzi, Jin destroyed the Luhun Rong, bandits murdered the elder brother of the marquis of Wei, the rulers of Cai and Ju fled, Wu destroyed Chao, Prince Guang killed King Liao, and three ministers of Song rebelled against their ruler with their fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Chu killed the ruler of Rongmanzi, Jin destroyed the Luhun Rong, bandits murdered the elder brother of the marquis of Wei, the rulers of Cai and Ju fled, Wu destroyed Chao, Prince Guang killed King Liao, and three ministers of Song rebelled against their ruler with their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 它如仲舒。

translations.literal: Other points are as in Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: Other points are as in Zhongshu.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為二日魯、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Lu and Zhao on the second day.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Lu and Zhao on the second day.

sentences.zh: 是月斗建辰。

translations.literal: That month Jupiter was in Chen.
translations.idiomatic: That month Jupiter was in Chen.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳梓慎曰：「將大水。」

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says Zishen said: "There will be great flooding."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says Zishen said: "There will be great flooding."

sentences.zh: 昭子曰：「旱也。

translations.literal: Zhaozi said: "It will be drought.
translations.idiomatic: Zhaozi said: "It will be drought.

sentences.zh: 日過分而陽猶不克，克必甚，能無旱乎！

translations.literal: The sun has passed its division and yet yang still cannot prevail; when it does prevail it will be severe. How could there not be drought?
translations.idiomatic: The sun has passed its division and yet yang still cannot prevail; when it does prevail it will be severe. How could there not be drought?

sentences.zh: 陽不克，莫將積聚也。」

translations.literal: If yang does not prevail, nothing can accumulate."
translations.idiomatic: If yang does not prevail, nothing can accumulate."

sentences.zh: 是歲秋，大雩，旱也。

translations.literal: That autumn there was Great Yu rain-prayer: it was drought.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn there was Great Yu rain-prayer: it was drought.

sentences.zh: 二至二分，日有食之，不為災。

translations.literal: At the two solstices and two equinoxes, if there is a solar eclipse it is not counted as disaster.
translations.idiomatic: At the two solstices and two equinoxes, if there is a solar eclipse it is not counted as disaster.

sentences.zh: 日月之行也，春秋分日夜等，故同道；

translations.literal: In the movement of sun and moon, at spring and autumn equinox day and night are equal, so their paths coincide;
translations.idiomatic: In the movement of sun and moon, at spring and autumn equinox day and night are equal, so their paths coincide;

sentences.zh: 冬夏至長短極，故相過。

translations.literal: at winter and summer solstice length and shortness reach extremes, so they pass one another.
translations.idiomatic: at winter and summer solstice length and shortness reach extremes, so they pass one another.

sentences.zh: 相過同道而食輕，不為大災，水旱而已。

translations.literal: When they pass and eclipse on coincident paths, the eclipse is light and not a great disaster, only flood or drought.
translations.idiomatic: When they pass and eclipse on coincident paths, the eclipse is light and not a great disaster, only flood or drought.

sentences.zh: 三十一年「十二月辛亥朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year thirty-one: "On xinhai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year thirty-one: "On xinhai, first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在心，天子象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart, symbol of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Heart, symbol of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 時京師微弱，後諸侯果相率而城周，宋中幾亡尊天子之心，而不衰城。

translations.literal: At that time the royal capital was weak; later feudal lords indeed jointly fortified Zhou. Song nearly lost all respect for the Son of Heaven and did not repair walls.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the royal capital was weak; later feudal lords indeed jointly fortified Zhou. Song nearly lost all respect for the Son of Heaven and did not repair walls.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時吳滅徐，而蔡滅沈，楚圍蔡，吳敗楚入郢，昭王走出。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that then Wu destroyed Xu, Cai destroyed Shen, Chu besieged Cai, Wu defeated Chu and entered Ying, and King Zhao fled.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that then Wu destroyed Xu, Cai destroyed Shen, Chu besieged Cai, Wu defeated Chu and entered Ying, and King Zhao fled.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為二日宋、燕分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Song and Yan on the second day.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the sectors of Song and Yan on the second day.

sentences.zh: 定公五年「三月辛亥朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ding year five: "On xinhai, first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ding year five: "On xinhai, first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後鄭滅許，魯陽虎作亂，竊寶玉大弓，季桓子退仲尼，宋三臣以邑叛。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Zheng destroyed Xu, Yang Hu of Lu made disorder and stole treasured jade and great bow, Ji Huanzi dismissed Confucius, and three ministers of Song rebelled with their towns.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward Zheng destroyed Xu, Yang Hu of Lu made disorder and stole treasured jade and great bow, Ji Huanzi dismissed Confucius, and three ministers of Song rebelled with their towns.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為正月二日燕、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of first month, sectors of Yan and Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of first month, sectors of Yan and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 十二年「十一月丙寅朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year twelve: "On bingyin, first day of eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year twelve: "On bingyin, first day of eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為後晉三大夫以邑叛，薛弒其君，楚滅頓、胡，越敗吳，衛逐世子。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward three grandees of Jin rebelled with their towns, Xue regicide-killed its ruler, Chu destroyed Dun and Hu, Yue defeated Wu, and Wei expelled its heir.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that afterward three grandees of Jin rebelled with their towns, Xue regicide-killed its ruler, Chu destroyed Dun and Hu, Yue defeated Wu, and Wei expelled its heir.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為十二月二日楚、鄭分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of twelfth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of twelfth month, sectors of Chu and Zheng.

sentences.zh: 十五年「八月庚辰朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "On gengchen, first day of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "On gengchen, first day of eighth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宿在柳，周室大壞，夷狄主諸夏之象也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Willow, image of the Zhou house greatly collapsing and the border peoples ruling the Xia states.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held the lodge was Willow, image of the Zhou house greatly collapsing and the border peoples ruling the Xia states.

sentences.zh: 明年，中國諸侯果累累從楚而圍蔡，蔡恐，遷于州來。

translations.literal: The next year, feudal lords of the central states indeed followed Chu in numbers to besiege Cai; Cai, fearful, moved to Zhoulai.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, feudal lords of the central states indeed followed Chu in numbers to besiege Cai; Cai, fearful, moved to Zhoulai.

sentences.zh: 晉人執戎蠻子歸于楚，京師楚也。

translations.literal: Men of Jin captured Rongmanzi and returned him to Chu; the capital authority was now Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Men of Jin captured Rongmanzi and returned him to Chu; the capital authority was now Chu.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為盜殺蔡侯，齊陳乞弒其君而立陽生，孔子終不用。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that bandits killed the marquis of Cai, Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler and installed Yangsheng, and Confucius was never finally employed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that bandits killed the marquis of Cai, Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler and installed Yangsheng, and Confucius was never finally employed.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為六月晉、趙分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Jin and Zhao in sixth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was sectors of Jin and Zhao in sixth month.

sentences.zh: 哀公十四年「五月庚申朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ai year fourteen: "On gengshen, first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year fourteen: "On gengshen, first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed."

sentences.zh: 在獲麟後。

translations.literal: This was after the capture of the qilin.
translations.idiomatic: This was after the capture of the qilin.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為三月二日齊、衛分。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held this was the second day of third month, sectors of Qi and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held this was the second day of third month, sectors of Qi and Wei.

sentences.zh: 凡春秋十二公，二百四十二年，日食三十六。

translations.literal: Across the twelve dukes of Spring and Autumn, over 242 years, there were 36 solar eclipses.
translations.idiomatic: Across the twelve dukes of Spring and Autumn, over 242 years, there were 36 solar eclipses.

sentences.zh: 穀梁以為朔二十六，晦七，夜二，二日一。

translations.literal: Guliang counts 26 on first day, 7 at month-end, 2 night eclipses, and 1 lasting two days.
translations.idiomatic: Guliang counts 26 on first day, 7 at month-end, 2 night eclipses, and 1 lasting two days.

sentences.zh: 公羊以為朔二十七，二日七，晦二。

translations.literal: Gongyang counts 27 on first day, 7 lasting two days, and 2 at month-end.
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang counts 27 on first day, 7 lasting two days, and 2 at month-end.

sentences.zh: 左氏以為朔十六，二日十八，晦一，不書日者二。

translations.literal: Zuo counts 16 on first day, 18 lasting two days, 1 at month-end, and 2 without day recorded.
translations.idiomatic: Zuo counts 16 on first day, 18 lasting two days, 1 at month-end, and 2 without day recorded.

sentences.zh: 高帝三年十月甲戌晦，日有食之，在斗二十度，燕地也。

translations.literal: In tenth month of year three under Emperor Gao, on jiaxu at month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 20 degrees of Dou, territory of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: In tenth month of year three under Emperor Gao, on jiaxu at month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 20 degrees of Dou, territory of Yan.

sentences.zh: 後二年，燕王臧荼反，誅，立盧綰為燕王，後又反，敗。

translations.literal: Two years later, King Zang Tu of Yan rebelled and was executed; Lu Wan was made king of Yan and later also rebelled and was defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, King Zang Tu of Yan rebelled and was executed; Lu Wan was made king of Yan and later also rebelled and was defeated.

sentences.zh: 十一月癸卯晦，日有食之，在虛三度，齊地也。

translations.literal: On guimao at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 3 degrees of Xu, territory of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 3 degrees of Xu, territory of Qi.

sentences.zh: 後二年，齊王韓信徙為楚王，明年廢為列侯，後又反，誅。

translations.literal: Two years later King Han Xin of Qi was moved to be king of Chu; next year he was reduced to marquis, later rebelled, and was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later King Han Xin of Qi was moved to be king of Chu; next year he was reduced to marquis, later rebelled, and was executed.

sentences.zh: 九年六月乙未晦，日有食之，既，在張十三度。

translations.literal: In sixth month of year nine, on yiwei at month-end, the sun was eclipsed, total, at 13 degrees of Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of year nine, on yiwei at month-end, the sun was eclipsed, total, at 13 degrees of Zhang.

sentences.zh: 惠帝七年正月辛丑朔，日有食之，在危十三度。

translations.literal: In first month of Emperor Hui year seven, on xinchou first day, the sun was eclipsed at 13 degrees of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Emperor Hui year seven, on xinchou first day, the sun was eclipsed at 13 degrees of Wei.

sentences.zh: 谷永以為歲首正月朔日，是為三朝，尊者惡之。

translations.literal: Gu Yong held that at year's first month first day, one of the three court mornings, the honored should fear it.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong held that at year's first month first day, one of the three court mornings, the honored should fear it.

sentences.zh: 五月丁卯，先晦一日，日有食之，幾盡，在七星初。

translations.literal: On dingmao of fifth month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed nearly to totality at the beginning of Seven Stars.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao of fifth month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed nearly to totality at the beginning of Seven Stars.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為五月微陰始起而犯至陽，其占重。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that in fifth month slight yin first rises and infringes utmost yang, so this divination is weighty.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in fifth month slight yin first rises and infringes utmost yang, so this divination is weighty.

sentences.zh: 至其八月，宮車晏駕，有呂氏詐置嗣君之害。

translations.literal: By that eighth month, the imperial carriage had passed away, and there was the Lü clan's false placement of a successor.
translations.idiomatic: By that eighth month, the imperial carriage had passed away, and there was the Lü clan's false placement of a successor.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「凡日食不以晦朔者，名曰薄。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Any eclipse not at new moon or month-end is called bo (encroachment).
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Any eclipse not at new moon or month-end is called bo (encroachment).

sentences.zh: 人君誅將不以理，或賊臣將暴起，日月雖不同宿，陰氣盛，薄日光也。」

translations.literal: The ruler will punish generals without due principle, or traitor-ministers will suddenly rise. Though sun and moon are in different lodges, yin qi is excessive and dims the sun's light."
translations.idiomatic: The ruler will punish generals without due principle, or traitor-ministers will suddenly rise. Though sun and moon are in different lodges, yin qi is excessive and dims the sun's light."

sentences.zh: 高后二年六月丙戌晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In second year of Empress Gao, on bingxu at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In second year of Empress Gao, on bingxu at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 七年正月己丑晦，日有食之，既，在營室九度，為宮室中。

translations.literal: In year seven, on jichou at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed, total, at 9 degrees of Encampment, within palace-sector.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, on jichou at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed, total, at 9 degrees of Encampment, within palace-sector.

sentences.zh: 時高后惡之，曰：「此為我也！」

translations.literal: At that time Empress Gao disliked it, saying: "This is for me!"
translations.idiomatic: At that time Empress Gao disliked it, saying: "This is for me!"

sentences.zh: 明年應。

translations.literal: The next year it was fulfilled.
translations.idiomatic: The next year it was fulfilled.

sentences.zh: 文帝二年十一月癸卯晦，日有食之，在婺女一度。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wen year two, on guimao at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 1 degree of Wunü.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen year two, on guimao at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 1 degree of Wunü.

sentences.zh: 三年十月丁酉晦，日有食之，在斗二十三度。

translations.literal: In year three, on dingyou at month-end in tenth month, the sun was eclipsed at 23 degrees of Dou.
translations.idiomatic: In year three, on dingyou at month-end in tenth month, the sun was eclipsed at 23 degrees of Dou.

sentences.zh: 十一月丁卯晦，日有食之，在虛八度。

translations.literal: In eleventh month, on dingmao at month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Xu.
translations.idiomatic: In eleventh month, on dingmao at month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Xu.

sentences.zh: 後四年四月丙辰晦，日有食之，在東井十三度。

translations.literal: Four years later, on bingchen at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 13 degrees of Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later, on bingchen at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 13 degrees of Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 七年正月辛未朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year seven, on xinwei first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, on xinwei first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 景帝三年二月壬午晦，日有食之，在胃二度。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing year three, on renwu at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed at 2 degrees of Stomach.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing year three, on renwu at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed at 2 degrees of Stomach.

sentences.zh: 七年十一月庚寅晦，日有食之，在虛九度。

translations.literal: In year seven, on gengyin at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 9 degrees of Xu.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, on gengyin at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 9 degrees of Xu.

sentences.zh: 中元年十二月甲寅晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Zhongyuan year one, on jiayin at month-end in twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongyuan year one, on jiayin at month-end in twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 中二年九月甲戌晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Zhongyuan year two, on jiaxu at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongyuan year two, on jiaxu at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 三年九月戊戌晦，日有食之，幾盡，在尾九度。

translations.literal: In year three, on wuxu at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed nearly total at 9 degrees of Tail.
translations.idiomatic: In year three, on wuxu at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed nearly total at 9 degrees of Tail.

sentences.zh: 六年七月辛亥晦，日有食之，在軫七度。

translations.literal: In year six, on xinhai at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 7 degrees of Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: In year six, on xinhai at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 7 degrees of Zhen.

sentences.zh: 後元年七月乙巳，先晦一日，日有食之，在翼十七度。

translations.literal: In Houyuan year one, on yisi in seventh month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 17 degrees of Wing.
translations.idiomatic: In Houyuan year one, on yisi in seventh month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 17 degrees of Wing.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元二年二月丙戌朔，日有食之，在奎十四度。
