translations.literal: In Jianyuan year two under Emperor Wu, on bingxu first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed at 14 degrees of Kui.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianyuan year two under Emperor Wu, on bingxu first day of second month, the sun was eclipsed at 14 degrees of Kui.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為奎為卑賤婦人，後有衛皇后自至微興，卒有不終之害。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held Kui signifies low-status women; later Empress Wei rose from very humble origins and in the end suffered harm before natural completion.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held Kui signifies low-status women; later Empress Wei rose from very humble origins and in the end suffered harm before natural completion.

sentences.zh: 三年九月丙子晦，日有食之，在尾二度。

translations.literal: In year three, on bingzi at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed at 2 degrees of Tail.
translations.idiomatic: In year three, on bingzi at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed at 2 degrees of Tail.

sentences.zh: 五年正月己巳朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year five, on jisi first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year five, on jisi first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 元光元年二月丙辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Yuanguang year one, on bingchen at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanguang year one, on bingchen at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 七月癸未，先晦一日，日有食之，在翼八度。

translations.literal: On guiwei in seventh month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Wing.
translations.idiomatic: On guiwei in seventh month, one day before month-end, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Wing.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為前年高園便殿災，與春秋御廩災後日食於翼、軫同。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that the previous year's fire at the side hall of Gao Garden corresponded with the Spring and Autumn pattern of granary-fire followed by eclipse in Wing and Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that the previous year's fire at the side hall of Gao Garden corresponded with the Spring and Autumn pattern of granary-fire followed by eclipse in Wing and Zhen.

sentences.zh: 其占，內有女變，外為諸侯。

translations.literal: Its divination: internally female change, externally troubles from feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Its divination: internally female change, externally troubles from feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 其後陳皇后廢，江都、淮南、衡山王謀反，誅。

translations.literal: Afterward Empress Chen was deposed, and the kings of Jiangdu, Huainan, and Hengshan plotted rebellion and were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Chen was deposed, and the kings of Jiangdu, Huainan, and Hengshan plotted rebellion and were executed.

sentences.zh: 日中時食從東北，過半，晡時復。

translations.literal: At noon the eclipse began from the northeast, passed half, and recovered by late afternoon.
translations.idiomatic: At noon the eclipse began from the northeast, passed half, and recovered by late afternoon.

sentences.zh: 元朔二年二月乙巳晦，日有食之，在胃三度。

translations.literal: In Yuanshuo year two, on yisi at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed at 3 degrees of Stomach.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshuo year two, on yisi at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed at 3 degrees of Stomach.

sentences.zh: 六年十一月癸丑晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year six, on guichou at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year six, on guichou at month-end in eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 元狩元年五月乙巳晦，日有食之，在柳六度。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou year one, on yisi at month-end in fifth month, the sun was eclipsed at 6 degrees of Willow.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou year one, on yisi at month-end in fifth month, the sun was eclipsed at 6 degrees of Willow.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳推以為是時日食從旁右，法曰君失臣。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition infers that at this time the eclipse came from the right side; by rule this means ruler losing ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition infers that at this time the eclipse came from the right side; by rule this means ruler losing ministers.

sentences.zh: 明年丞相公孫弘薨。

translations.literal: The next year Chancellor Gongsun Hong died.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Chancellor Gongsun Hong died.

sentences.zh: 日食從旁左者，亦君失臣；

translations.literal: If the eclipse comes from the left side, this also means ruler losing ministers;
translations.idiomatic: If the eclipse comes from the left side, this also means ruler losing ministers;

sentences.zh: 從上者，臣失君；

translations.literal: from above means ministers losing the ruler;
translations.idiomatic: from above means ministers losing the ruler;

sentences.zh: 從下者，君失民。

translations.literal: from below means ruler losing the people.
translations.idiomatic: from below means ruler losing the people.

sentences.zh: 元鼎五年四月丁丑晦，日有食之，在東井二十三度。

translations.literal: In Yuanding year five, on dingchou at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 23 degrees of Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanding year five, on dingchou at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 23 degrees of Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 元封四年六月己酉朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Yuanfeng year four, on jiyou first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanfeng year four, on jiyou first day of sixth month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 太始元年正月乙巳晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Taishi year one, on yisi at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Taishi year one, on yisi at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 四年十月甲寅晦，日有食之，在斗十九度。

translations.literal: In year four, on jiayin at month-end in tenth month, the sun was eclipsed at 19 degrees of Dou.
translations.idiomatic: In year four, on jiayin at month-end in tenth month, the sun was eclipsed at 19 degrees of Dou.

sentences.zh: 征和四年八月辛酉晦，日有食之，不盡如鉤，在亢二度。

translations.literal: In Zhenghe year four, on xinyou at month-end in eighth month, the sun was eclipsed, not total and hook-shaped, at 2 degrees of Kang.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhenghe year four, on xinyou at month-end in eighth month, the sun was eclipsed, not total and hook-shaped, at 2 degrees of Kang.

sentences.zh: 晡時食從西北，日下晡時復。

translations.literal: By late afternoon the eclipse came from the northwest, and recovered as the sun lowered in late afternoon.
translations.idiomatic: By late afternoon the eclipse came from the northwest, and recovered as the sun lowered in late afternoon.

sentences.zh: 昭帝始元三年十一月壬辰朔，日有食之，在斗九度，燕地也。

translations.literal: In Shiyuan year three under Emperor Zhao, on renchen first day of eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 9 degrees of Dou, territory of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: In Shiyuan year three under Emperor Zhao, on renchen first day of eleventh month, the sun was eclipsed at 9 degrees of Dou, territory of Yan.

sentences.zh: 後四年，燕剌王謀反，誅。

translations.literal: Four years later the Prince of Yan plotted rebellion and was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later the Prince of Yan plotted rebellion and was executed.

sentences.zh: 元鳳元年七月己亥晦，日有食之，幾盡，在張十二度。

translations.literal: In Yuanfeng year one, on jihai at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed nearly to totality at 12 degrees of Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanfeng year one, on jihai at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed nearly to totality at 12 degrees of Zhang.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為己亥而既，其占重。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that because it was jihai and nearly total, its divination was weighty.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that because it was jihai and nearly total, its divination was weighty.

sentences.zh: 後六年，宮車晏駕，卒以亡嗣。

translations.literal: Six years later the imperial carriage passed away and the line ended without heir.
translations.idiomatic: Six years later the imperial carriage passed away and the line ended without heir.

sentences.zh: 宣帝地節元年十二月癸亥晦，日有食之，在營室十五度。

translations.literal: In Dijie year one under Emperor Xuan, on guihai at month-end in twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed at 15 degrees of Encampment.
translations.idiomatic: In Dijie year one under Emperor Xuan, on guihai at month-end in twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed at 15 degrees of Encampment.

sentences.zh: 五鳳元年十二月乙酉朔，日有食之，在婺女十度。

translations.literal: In Wufeng year one, on yiyou first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed at 10 degrees of Wunü.
translations.idiomatic: In Wufeng year one, on yiyou first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed at 10 degrees of Wunü.

sentences.zh: 四年四月辛丑朔，日有食之，在畢十九度。

translations.literal: In year four, on xinchou first day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 19 degrees of Bi.
translations.idiomatic: In year four, on xinchou first day of fourth month, the sun was eclipsed at 19 degrees of Bi.

sentences.zh: 是為正月朔，慝未作，左氏以為重異。

translations.literal: This was first day of proper month before hidden evils arose; Zuo regarded it as a grave anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: This was first day of proper month before hidden evils arose; Zuo regarded it as a grave anomaly.

sentences.zh: 元帝永光二年三月壬戌朔，日有食之，在婁八度。

translations.literal: In Yongguang year two under Emperor Yuan, on renxu first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Lou.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongguang year two under Emperor Yuan, on renxu first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed at 8 degrees of Lou.

sentences.zh: 四年六月戊寅晦，日有食之，在張七度。

translations.literal: In year four, on wuyin at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed at 7 degrees of Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: In year four, on wuyin at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed at 7 degrees of Zhang.

sentences.zh: 建昭五年六月壬申晦，日有食之，不盡如鉤，因入。

translations.literal: In Jianzhao year five, on renshen at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, then entered cloud.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianzhao year five, on renshen at month-end in sixth month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, then entered cloud.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始三年十二月戊申朔，日有食之，其夜未央殿中地震。

translations.literal: In Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, on wushen first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed; that night the ground shook inside Weiyang Hall.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, on wushen first day of twelfth month, the sun was eclipsed; that night the ground shook inside Weiyang Hall.

sentences.zh: 谷永對曰：「日食婺女九度，占在皇后。

translations.literal: Gu Yong replied: "An eclipse at 9 degrees of Wunü is a divination aimed at the empress.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong replied: "An eclipse at 9 degrees of Wunü is a divination aimed at the empress.

sentences.zh: 地震蕭牆之內，咎在貴妾。

translations.literal: An earthquake inside the inner walls places blame on favored consorts.
translations.idiomatic: An earthquake inside the inner walls places blame on favored consorts.

sentences.zh: 二者俱發，明同事異人，共掩制陽，將害繼嗣也。

translations.literal: The two occurring together makes clear one affair involving different persons: together they would cover and constrain yang and harm succession.
translations.idiomatic: The two occurring together makes clear one affair involving different persons: together they would cover and constrain yang and harm succession.

sentences.zh: 亶日食，則妾不見；

translations.literal: If only an eclipse occurs, the consort is not implicated;
translations.idiomatic: If only an eclipse occurs, the consort is not implicated;

sentences.zh: 亶地震，則后不見。

translations.literal: if only an earthquake occurs, the empress is not implicated.
translations.idiomatic: if only an earthquake occurs, the empress is not implicated.

sentences.zh: 異日而發，則似殊事；

translations.literal: If they occur on different days, they seem separate matters;
translations.idiomatic: If they occur on different days, they seem separate matters;

sentences.zh: 亡故動變，則恐不知。

translations.literal: if change stirs without cause, people may fail to understand.
translations.idiomatic: if change stirs without cause, people may fail to understand.

sentences.zh: 是月后妾當有失節之郵，故天因此兩見其變。

translations.literal: In this month empress and consorts should have had a failing in proper conduct, so Heaven displayed both anomalies through this.
translations.idiomatic: In this month empress and consorts should have had a failing in proper conduct, so Heaven displayed both anomalies through this.

sentences.zh: 若曰，違失婦道，隔遠眾妾，妨絕繼嗣者，此二人也。」

translations.literal: It is as if saying: those who violate women's duty, keep the many consorts apart, and block succession are these two women."
translations.idiomatic: It is as if saying: those who violate women's duty, keep the many consorts apart, and block succession are these two women."

sentences.zh: 杜欽對亦曰：「日以戊申食，時加未。

translations.literal: Du Qin likewise replied: "The eclipse fell on wushen with the hour at wei.
translations.idiomatic: Du Qin likewise replied: "The eclipse fell on wushen with the hour at wei.

sentences.zh: 戊未，土也，中宮之部。

translations.literal: Wu and wei are earth, the central palace sector.
translations.idiomatic: Wu and wei are earth, the central palace sector.

sentences.zh: 其夜殿中地震，此必適妾將有爭寵相害而為患者。

translations.literal: An earthquake in the palace that night means the principal wife and favored concubine would contend for favor and harm each other, becoming calamity.
translations.idiomatic: An earthquake in the palace that night means the principal wife and favored concubine would contend for favor and harm each other, becoming calamity.

sentences.zh: 人事失於下，變象見於上。

translations.literal: When human affairs fail below, changing signs appear above.
translations.idiomatic: When human affairs fail below, changing signs appear above.

sentences.zh: 能應之司德，則咎異消；

translations.literal: If one responds through governance in virtue, blame and anomaly are dispelled;
translations.idiomatic: If one responds through governance in virtue, blame and anomaly are dispelled;

sentences.zh: 忽而不戒，則禍敗至。

translations.literal: if one neglects warning, disaster and ruin arrive.
translations.idiomatic: if one neglects warning, disaster and ruin arrive.

sentences.zh: 應之，非誠不立，非信不行。」

translations.literal: To respond, sincerity must stand and trust must be carried out."
translations.idiomatic: To respond, sincerity must stand and trust must be carried out."

sentences.zh: 河平元年四月己亥晦，日有食之，不盡如鉤，在東井六度。

translations.literal: In Heping year one, on jihai at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, at 6 degrees of Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping year one, on jihai at month-end in fourth month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, at 6 degrees of Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 劉向對曰：「四月交於五月，月同孝惠，日同孝昭。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang replied: "The fourth month crossing into fifth shares month with Emperor Xiaohui and day with Emperor Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang replied: "The fourth month crossing into fifth shares month with Emperor Xiaohui and day with Emperor Zhao.

sentences.zh: 東井，京師地，且既，其占恐害繼嗣。」

translations.literal: Eastern Well is the capital's sector, and with this near-totality the divination likely threatens succession."
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Well is the capital's sector, and with this near-totality the divination likely threatens succession."

sentences.zh: 日蚤食時，從西南起。

translations.literal: The eclipse began early in daylight from the southwest.
translations.idiomatic: The eclipse began early in daylight from the southwest.

sentences.zh: 三年八月乙卯晦，日有食之，在房。

translations.literal: In year three, on yimao at month-end in eighth month, the sun was eclipsed in Fang.
translations.idiomatic: In year three, on yimao at month-end in eighth month, the sun was eclipsed in Fang.

sentences.zh: 四年三月癸丑朔，日有食之，在昴。

translations.literal: In year four, on guichou first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed in Mao.
translations.idiomatic: In year four, on guichou first day of third month, the sun was eclipsed in Mao.

sentences.zh: 陽朔元年二月丁未晦，日有食之，在胃。

translations.literal: In Yangshuo year one, on dingwei at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed in Stomach.
translations.idiomatic: In Yangshuo year one, on dingwei at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed in Stomach.

sentences.zh: 永始元年九月丁巳晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Yongshi year one, on dingsi at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongshi year one, on dingsi at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 谷永以京房易占對曰：「元年九月日蝕，酒亡節之所致也。

translations.literal: Gu Yong, using Jing Fang's Yi divination, replied: "The eclipse in ninth month of year one was caused by lack of restraint in drink.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong, using Jing Fang's Yi divination, replied: "The eclipse in ninth month of year one was caused by lack of restraint in drink.

sentences.zh: 獨使京師知之，四國不見者，若曰，湛湎于酒，君臣不別，禍在內也。」

translations.literal: That only the capital knew while the four quarters did not see it is as if saying: sunk in drink and with no distinction between ruler and ministers, the calamity lies within."
translations.idiomatic: That only the capital knew while the four quarters did not see it is as if saying: sunk in drink and with no distinction between ruler and ministers, the calamity lies within."

sentences.zh: 永始二年二月乙酉晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Yongshi year two, on yiyou at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongshi year two, on yiyou at month-end in second month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 谷永以京房易占對曰：「今年二月日食，賦斂不得度，民愁怨之所致也。

translations.literal: Gu Yong, using Jing Fang's Yi divination, replied: "This year's second-month eclipse came from taxation and levies exceeding measure, producing popular sorrow and resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong, using Jing Fang's Yi divination, replied: "This year's second-month eclipse came from taxation and levies exceeding measure, producing popular sorrow and resentment.

sentences.zh: 所以使四方皆見，京師陰蔽者，若曰，人君好治宮室，大營墳墓，賦斂茲重，而百姓屈竭，禍在外也。」

translations.literal: That all directions saw it while the capital was clouded is as if saying: the ruler delights in building palaces and grand tombs, exactions grow heavier, the people are exhausted and bent low, and the calamity lies outside."
translations.idiomatic: That all directions saw it while the capital was clouded is as if saying: the ruler delights in building palaces and grand tombs, exactions grow heavier, the people are exhausted and bent low, and the calamity lies outside."

sentences.zh: 三年正月己卯晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year three, on jimao at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year three, on jimao at month-end in first month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 四年七月辛未晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year four, on xinwei at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year four, on xinwei at month-end in seventh month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 元延元年正月己亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In Yuanyan year one, on jihai first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanyan year one, on jihai first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 哀帝元壽元年正月辛丑朔，日有食之，不盡如鉤，在營室十度，與惠帝七年同月日。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou year one under Emperor Ai, on xinchou first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, at 10 degrees of Encampment, same month and day as Emperor Hui year seven.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou year one under Emperor Ai, on xinchou first day of first month, the sun was eclipsed, incomplete and hook-shaped, at 10 degrees of Encampment, same month and day as Emperor Hui year seven.

sentences.zh: 二年三月壬辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In year two, on renchen at month-end in third month, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: In year two, on renchen at month-end in third month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始元年五月丁巳朔，日有食之，在東井。

translations.literal: In Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, on dingsi first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed in Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, on dingsi first day of fifth month, the sun was eclipsed in Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 二年九月戊申晦，日有食之，既。

translations.literal: In year two, on wushen at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed, total.
translations.idiomatic: In year two, on wushen at month-end in ninth month, the sun was eclipsed, total.

sentences.zh: 凡漢著紀十二世，二百一十二年，日食五十三，朔十四，晦三十六，先晦一日三。

translations.literal: Across twelve recorded Han reigns over 212 years, there were fifty-three solar eclipses: fourteen on new moon, thirty-six at month-end, and three one day before month-end.
translations.idiomatic: Across twelve recorded Han reigns over 212 years, there were fifty-three solar eclipses: fourteen on new moon, thirty-six at month-end, and three one day before month-end.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始元年八月戊午，晨漏未盡三刻，有兩月重見。

translations.literal: In Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, on wuwu in eighth month, at dawn before the water-clock had finished three quarters, two moons were seen together.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, on wuwu in eighth month, at dawn before the water-clock had finished three quarters, two moons were seen together.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「『婦貞厲，月幾望，君子征，凶。』

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""A woman's constancy is peril; the moon almost full; if a gentleman campaigns, misfortune."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""A woman's constancy is peril; the moon almost full; if a gentleman campaigns, misfortune."

sentences.zh: 言君弱而婦彊，為陰所乘，則月並出。

translations.literal: It means when ruler is weak and wife strong, with yin riding over it, then double moons appear.
translations.idiomatic: It means when ruler is weak and wife strong, with yin riding over it, then double moons appear.

sentences.zh: 晦而月見西方謂之朓，朔而月見東方謂之仄慝，仄慝則侯王其肅，朓則侯王其舒。」

translations.literal: Seeing the moon in the west at month-end is called tiao; seeing the moon in the east at new moon is called ze-te. In ze-te, feudal lords should be stern; in tiao, feudal lords should be relaxed."
translations.idiomatic: Seeing the moon in the west at month-end is called tiao; seeing the moon in the east at new moon is called ze-te. In ze-te, feudal lords should be stern; in tiao, feudal lords should be relaxed."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為朓者疾也，君舒緩則臣驕慢，故日行遲而月行疾也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that tiao means haste: when ruler is lax, ministers grow arrogant, so the sun moves slowly and moon quickly.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that tiao means haste: when ruler is lax, ministers grow arrogant, so the sun moves slowly and moon quickly.

sentences.zh: 仄慝者不進之意，君肅急則臣恐懼，故日行疾而月行遲，不敢迫近君也。

translations.literal: Ze-te means unwilling to advance: when ruler is severe and urgent, ministers are fearful, so the sun moves quickly and moon slowly, not daring to press close to the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Ze-te means unwilling to advance: when ruler is severe and urgent, ministers are fearful, so the sun moves quickly and moon slowly, not daring to press close to the ruler.

sentences.zh: 不舒不急，以正失之者，食朔日。

translations.literal: When one is neither lax nor urgent yet still misses the mean, eclipse comes on new-moon day.
translations.idiomatic: When one is neither lax nor urgent yet still misses the mean, eclipse comes on new-moon day.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為舒者侯王展意顓事，臣下促急，故月行疾也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that in "relaxed," feudal lords stretch intent and monopolize affairs while ministers below become tense and hurried, hence the moon moves fast.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that in "relaxed," feudal lords stretch intent and monopolize affairs while ministers below become tense and hurried, hence the moon moves fast.

sentences.zh: 肅者王侯縮朒不任事，臣下弛縱，故月行遲也。

translations.literal: In "stern," kings and lords shrink back and fail to take up affairs while ministers below become loose and indulgent, hence the moon moves slow.
translations.idiomatic: In "stern," kings and lords shrink back and fail to take up affairs while ministers below become loose and indulgent, hence the moon moves slow.

sentences.zh: 當春秋時，侯王率多縮朒不任事，故食二日仄慝者十八，食晦日朓者一，此其效也。

translations.literal: In the Spring and Autumn era, feudal rulers mostly shrank from responsibility; therefore eclipses lasting two days with ze-te were eighteen, while month-end tiao eclipses were one, this being the verification.
translations.idiomatic: In the Spring and Autumn era, feudal rulers mostly shrank from responsibility; therefore eclipses lasting two days with ze-te were eighteen, while month-end tiao eclipses were one, this being the verification.

sentences.zh: 考之漢家，食晦朓者三十六，終亡二日仄慝者，歆說信矣。

translations.literal: Examined against Han: month-end tiao eclipses are thirty-six, and there are no two-day ze-te eclipses at all; thus Xin's explanation is trustworthy.
translations.idiomatic: Examined against Han: month-end tiao eclipses are thirty-six, and there are no two-day ze-te eclipses at all; thus Xin's explanation is trustworthy.

sentences.zh: 此皆謂日月亂行者也。

translations.literal: All these are cases of sun and moon moving disorderly.
translations.idiomatic: All these are cases of sun and moon moving disorderly.

sentences.zh: 元帝永光元年四月，日色青白，亡景，正中時有景亡光。

translations.literal: In Yongguang year one under Emperor Yuan, in fourth month, the sun's color turned bluish-white with no shadow; at true noon there was shadow but no light.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongguang year one under Emperor Yuan, in fourth month, the sun's color turned bluish-white with no shadow; at true noon there was shadow but no light.

sentences.zh: 是夏寒，至九月，日乃有光。

translations.literal: That summer was cold; only by ninth month did the sun regain light.
translations.idiomatic: That summer was cold; only by ninth month did the sun regain light.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「美不上人，茲謂上弱，厥異日白，七日不溫。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When merit does not rise to people, this is called weak above; its anomaly is a white sun and seven days without warmth.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When merit does not rise to people, this is called weak above; its anomaly is a white sun and seven days without warmth.

sentences.zh: 順亡所制茲謂弱，日白六十日，物亡霜而死。

translations.literal: When compliance has no controlling principle, this is called weakness; the sun is white for sixty days, and things die without frost.
translations.idiomatic: When compliance has no controlling principle, this is called weakness; the sun is white for sixty days, and things die without frost.

sentences.zh: 天子親伐，茲謂不知，日白，體動而寒。

translations.literal: When the Son of Heaven campaigns personally, this is called not knowing; the sun is white, body trembling and cold.
translations.idiomatic: When the Son of Heaven campaigns personally, this is called not knowing; the sun is white, body trembling and cold.

sentences.zh: 弱而有任，茲謂不亡，日白不溫，明不動。

translations.literal: When weak yet still bearing burdens, this is called not perishing; sun white and not warm, brightness unmoving.
translations.idiomatic: When weak yet still bearing burdens, this is called not perishing; sun white and not warm, brightness unmoving.

sentences.zh: 辟鳗公行，茲謂不伸，厥異日黑，大風起，天無雲，日光晻。

translations.literal: When ruler's conduct is disordered and public, this is called not stretching forth; its anomaly is black sun, great wind rising, sky cloudless, sun dim.
translations.idiomatic: When ruler's conduct is disordered and public, this is called not stretching forth; its anomaly is black sun, great wind rising, sky cloudless, sun dim.

sentences.zh: 不難上政，茲謂見過，日黑居仄，大如彈丸。」

translations.literal: Not finding governance difficult is called seeing fault; the black sun sits to the side, as large as a pellet."
translations.idiomatic: Not finding governance difficult is called seeing fault; the black sun sits to the side, as large as a pellet."

sentences.zh: 成帝河平元年正月壬寅朔，日月俱在營室，時日出赤。

translations.literal: In Heping year one under Emperor Cheng, on renyin first day of first month, sun and moon were both in Encampment; at sunrise the sun was red.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping year one under Emperor Cheng, on renyin first day of first month, sun and moon were both in Encampment; at sunrise the sun was red.

sentences.zh: 二月癸未，日朝赤，且入又赤，夜月赤。

translations.literal: On guiwei in second month, the sun was red at dawn and red again at setting; at night the moon was red.
translations.idiomatic: On guiwei in second month, the sun was red at dawn and red again at setting; at night the moon was red.

sentences.zh: 甲申，日出赤如血，亡光，漏上四刻半，乃頗有光，燭地赤黃，食後乃復。

translations.literal: On jiashen, the sun rose blood-red without light; only after four and a half water-clock quarters did it gain some light, casting red-yellow on the ground, and only after eclipse did it recover.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen, the sun rose blood-red without light; only after four and a half water-clock quarters did it gain some light, casting red-yellow on the ground, and only after eclipse did it recover.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「辟不聞道茲謂亡，厥異日赤。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When rulers do not hear the Way, this is called extinction; its anomaly is a red sun."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When rulers do not hear the Way, this is called extinction; its anomaly is a red sun."

sentences.zh: 三月乙未，日出黃，有黑氣大如錢，居日中央。

translations.literal: On yiwei in third month, the sun rose yellow with a black vapor as large as a coin at its center.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei in third month, the sun rose yellow with a black vapor as large as a coin at its center.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「祭天不順茲謂逆，厥異日赤，其中黑。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If sacrifice to Heaven is not aligned, this is called rebellion; its anomaly is red sun with black in the center.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If sacrifice to Heaven is not aligned, this is called rebellion; its anomaly is red sun with black in the center.

sentences.zh: 聞善不予，茲謂失知，厥異日黃。」

translations.literal: Hearing the good but not granting it, this is called loss of knowledge; its anomaly is yellow sun."
translations.idiomatic: Hearing the good but not granting it, this is called loss of knowledge; its anomaly is yellow sun."

sentences.zh: 夫大人者，與天地合其德，與日月合其明，故聖王在上，總命群賢，以亮天功，則日之光明，五色備具，燭燿亡主；

translations.literal: Now the great person aligns virtue with Heaven and Earth and brightness with sun and moon. Thus when a sage king is above and gathers worthy ministers to complete Heaven's work, the sun's light is bright and complete in five colors, shining with no irregularity;
translations.idiomatic: Now the great person aligns virtue with Heaven and Earth and brightness with sun and moon. Thus when a sage king is above and gathers worthy ministers to complete Heaven's work, the sun's light is bright and complete in five colors, shining with no irregularity;

sentences.zh: 有主則為異，應行而變也。

translations.literal: if there is an irregularity, it is anomaly, a response and change to conduct.
translations.idiomatic: if there is an irregularity, it is anomaly, a response and change to conduct.

sentences.zh: 色不虛改，形不虛毀，觀日之五變，足以監矣。

translations.literal: Color does not change in vain, form is not damaged in vain. Observing the sun's five transformations is enough for scrutiny.
translations.idiomatic: Color does not change in vain, form is not damaged in vain. Observing the sun's five transformations is enough for scrutiny.

sentences.zh: 故曰「縣象著明，莫大乎日月」，此之謂也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said, "Of hanging signs made manifest, none are greater than sun and moon"; this is what it means.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore it is said, "Of hanging signs made manifest, none are greater than sun and moon"; this is what it means.

sentences.zh: 嚴公七年「四月辛卯夜，恆星不見，夜中星隕如雨」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year seven: "On xinchou night of fourth month, regular stars were not seen, and at midnight stars fell like rain."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year seven: "On xinchou night of fourth month, regular stars were not seen, and at midnight stars fell like rain."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為常星二十八宿者，人君之象也；

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the regular stars and twenty-eight lodges symbolize rulers;
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the regular stars and twenty-eight lodges symbolize rulers;

sentences.zh: 眾星，萬民之類也。

translations.literal: the multitude of stars are of the same class as the myriad people.
translations.idiomatic: the multitude of stars are of the same class as the myriad people.

sentences.zh: 列宿不見，象諸侯微也；

translations.literal: The lodges not appearing symbolizes feudal lords becoming weak;
translations.idiomatic: The lodges not appearing symbolizes feudal lords becoming weak;

sentences.zh: 眾星隕墜，民失其所也。

translations.literal: the many stars falling signifies people losing their place.
translations.idiomatic: the many stars falling signifies people losing their place.

sentences.zh: 夜中者，為中國也。

translations.literal: Midnight signifies the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Midnight signifies the central states.

sentences.zh: 不及地而復，象齊桓起而救存之也。

translations.literal: Not reaching the ground and then returning symbolizes Duke Huan of Qi rising to rescue and preserve them.
translations.idiomatic: Not reaching the ground and then returning symbolizes Duke Huan of Qi rising to rescue and preserve them.

sentences.zh: 鄉亡桓公，星遂至地，中國其良絕矣。

translations.literal: Once Duke Huan was gone, stars reached the ground; the central states' good order was truly cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Once Duke Huan was gone, stars reached the ground; the central states' good order was truly cut off.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為夜中者，言不得終性命，中道敗也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that "at midnight" means they could not complete their allotted lives and failed halfway.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that "at midnight" means they could not complete their allotted lives and failed halfway.

sentences.zh: 或曰象其叛也，言當中道叛其上也。

translations.literal: Another view says it symbolized rebellion, meaning they would rebel against superiors halfway through.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says it symbolized rebellion, meaning they would rebel against superiors halfway through.

sentences.zh: 天垂象以視下，將欲人君防惡遠非，慎卑省微，以自全安也。

translations.literal: Heaven hangs down signs to show below, wishing rulers to guard against evil and keep far from wrong, be cautious in lowly beginnings and examine the subtle, thereby preserving their own safety.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven hangs down signs to show below, wishing rulers to guard against evil and keep far from wrong, be cautious in lowly beginnings and examine the subtle, thereby preserving their own safety.

sentences.zh: 如人君有賢明之材，畏天威命，若高宗謀祖己，成王泣金縢，改過修正，立信布德，存亡繼絕，修廢舉逸，下學而上達，裁什一之稅，復三日之役，節用儉服，以惠百姓，則諸侯懷德，士民歸仁，災消而福興矣。

translations.literal: If a ruler has worthy and clear ability, fears Heaven's commanding awe, like Gaozong consulting Zu Ji and King Cheng weeping over the metal-bound casket, correcting faults and rectifying conduct, establishing trust and spreading virtue, preserving what is about to perish and continuing what is cut off, restoring what is abandoned and raising the overlooked, learning below to reach above, reducing taxes to one-tenth, restoring three-day corvée, frugal use and plain dress to benefit the people, then feudal lords cherish virtue andscholars and commoners return to benevolence, so disasters vanish and blessings rise.
translations.idiomatic: If a ruler has worthy and clear ability, fears Heaven's commanding awe, like Gaozong consulting Zu Ji and King Cheng weeping over the metal-bound casket, correcting faults and rectifying conduct, establishing trust and spreading virtue, preserving what is about to perish and continuing what is cut off, restoring what is abandoned and raising the overlooked, learning below to reach above, reducing taxes to one-tenth, restoring three-day corvée, frugal use and plain dress to benefit the people, then feudal lords cherish virtue andscholars and commoners return to benevolence, so disasters vanish and blessings rise.

sentences.zh: 遂莫肯改寤，法則古人，而各行其私意，終於君臣乖離，上下交怨。

translations.literal: Yet none would awaken and reform: they invoked ancient models while each followed private intent, ending in ruler-minister estrangement and mutual resentment above and below.
translations.idiomatic: Yet none would awaken and reform: they invoked ancient models while each followed private intent, ending in ruler-minister estrangement and mutual resentment above and below.

sentences.zh: 自是之後，齊、宋之君弒，譚、遂、邢、衛之國滅，宿遷於宋，蔡獲於楚，晉相弒殺，五世乃定，此其效也。

translations.literal: After this, rulers of Qi and Song were regicide-killed; Tan, Sui, Xing, and Wei were destroyed; Su was moved to Song; Cai was captured by Chu; Jin ministers killed one another; only in the fifth generation did things settle. This was the effect.
translations.idiomatic: After this, rulers of Qi and Song were regicide-killed; Tan, Sui, Xing, and Wei were destroyed; Su was moved to Song; Cai was captured by Chu; Jin ministers killed one another; only in the fifth generation did things settle. This was the effect.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰：「恆星不見，夜明也；

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: "Regular stars not seen means the night was bright;
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: "Regular stars not seen means the night was bright;

sentences.zh: 星隕如雨，與雨偕也。」

translations.literal: stars falling like rain means they fell together with rain."
translations.idiomatic: stars falling like rain means they fell together with rain."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為晝象中國，夜象夷狄。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held day images the central states, and night images the border peoples.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held day images the central states, and night images the border peoples.

sentences.zh: 夜明，故常見之星皆不見，象中國微也。

translations.literal: With night bright, the stars usually seen were all not seen, symbolizing weakness of the central states.
translations.idiomatic: With night bright, the stars usually seen were all not seen, symbolizing weakness of the central states.

sentences.zh: 「星隕如雨」，如，而也，星隕而且雨，故曰「與雨偕也」，明雨與星隕，兩變相成也。

translations.literal: In "stars fell like rain," ru means "and": stars fell and rain also fell, hence "fell together with rain," showing the two anomalies completed one another.
translations.idiomatic: In "stars fell like rain," ru means "and": stars fell and rain also fell, hence "fell together with rain," showing the two anomalies completed one another.

sentences.zh: 洪範曰：「庶民惟星。」

translations.literal: The Great Plan says: "The common people are the stars."
translations.idiomatic: The Great Plan says: "The common people are the stars."

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「雷雨作，解。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Thunder and rain arise: Release."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "Thunder and rain arise: Release."

sentences.zh: 是歲歲在玄枵，齊分野也。

translations.literal: That year Jupiter was in Xuanxiao, sector of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: That year Jupiter was in Xuanxiao, sector of Qi.

sentences.zh: 夜中而星隕，象庶民中離上也。

translations.literal: Stars falling at midnight symbolizes common people separating from superiors.
translations.idiomatic: Stars falling at midnight symbolizes common people separating from superiors.

sentences.zh: 雨以解過施，復從上下，象齊桓行伯，復興周室也。

translations.literal: Rain to release excessive measures and restore from above to below symbolizes Duke Huan of Qi practicing hegemony and reviving the Zhou house.
translations.idiomatic: Rain to release excessive measures and restore from above to below symbolizes Duke Huan of Qi practicing hegemony and reviving the Zhou house.

sentences.zh: 周四月，夏二月也，日在降婁，魯分野也。

translations.literal: Zhou fourth month is Xia second month, with the sun in Jianglou, sector of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou fourth month is Xia second month, with the sun in Jianglou, sector of Lu.

sentences.zh: 先是，衛侯朔奔齊，衛公子黔牟立，齊帥諸侯伐之，天子使使救衛。

translations.literal: Before this, Marquis Shuo of Wei fled to Qi and Prince Qianmou was established in Wei; Qi led feudal lords to attack him, and the Son of Heaven sent envoys to rescue Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Marquis Shuo of Wei fled to Qi and Prince Qianmou was established in Wei; Qi led feudal lords to attack him, and the Son of Heaven sent envoys to rescue Wei.

sentences.zh: 魯公子溺專政，會齊以犯王命，嚴弗能止，卒從而伐衛，逐天王所立。

translations.literal: Prince Ni of Lu monopolized governance and joined Qi to violate royal command. Duke Zhuang could not stop him and ultimately joined in attacking Wei, expelling the ruler set up by the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Ni of Lu monopolized governance and joined Qi to violate royal command. Duke Zhuang could not stop him and ultimately joined in attacking Wei, expelling the ruler set up by the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 不義至甚，而自以為功。

translations.literal: The unrighteousness was extreme, yet he took it as merit.
translations.idiomatic: The unrighteousness was extreme, yet he took it as merit.

sentences.zh: 名去其上，政繇下作，尤著，故星隕於魯，天事常象也。

translations.literal: When the title loses what is above it, governance is made from below; this is especially manifest, so stars fell in Lu, the usual image of Heaven's affairs.
translations.idiomatic: When the title loses what is above it, governance is made from below; this is especially manifest, so stars fell in Lu, the usual image of Heaven's affairs.

sentences.zh: 成帝永始二年二月癸未，夜過中，星隕如雨，長一二丈，繹繹未至地滅，至雞鳴止。

translations.literal: In Yongshi year two under Emperor Cheng, on guiwei in second month, after midnight, stars fell like rain, each one or two zhang long, trailing in succession and vanishing before reaching ground, continuing until cockcrow.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongshi year two under Emperor Cheng, on guiwei in second month, after midnight, stars fell like rain, each one or two zhang long, trailing in succession and vanishing before reaching ground, continuing until cockcrow.

sentences.zh: 谷永對曰：「日月星辰燭臨下土，其有食隕之異，則遐邇幽隱靡不咸睹。

translations.literal: Gu Yong replied: "Sun, moon, and stars shine down upon the earth; when there are anomalies of eclipse or falling stars, near and far, hidden and open, all can see them.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong replied: "Sun, moon, and stars shine down upon the earth; when there are anomalies of eclipse or falling stars, near and far, hidden and open, all can see them.

sentences.zh: 星辰附離于天，猶庶民附離王者也。

translations.literal: Stars are attached and aligned with Heaven, just as common people are attached and aligned with the king.
translations.idiomatic: Stars are attached and aligned with Heaven, just as common people are attached and aligned with the king.

sentences.zh: 王者失道，綱紀廢頓，下將叛去，故星叛天而隕，以見其象。

translations.literal: When the king loses the Way and the framework of order collapses, those below will rebel and depart; therefore stars rebel from Heaven and fall, displaying the image.
translations.idiomatic: When the king loses the Way and the framework of order collapses, those below will rebel and depart; therefore stars rebel from Heaven and fall, displaying the image.

sentences.zh: 春秋記異，星隕最大，自魯嚴以來，至今再見。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn records anomalies, and star-fall is among the greatest. Since Duke Zhuang of Lu, it has appeared twice down to now.
translations.idiomatic: Spring and Autumn records anomalies, and star-fall is among the greatest. Since Duke Zhuang of Lu, it has appeared twice down to now.

sentences.zh: 臣聞三代所以喪亡者，皆繇婦人群小，湛湎於酒。

translations.literal: I have heard the reason the Three Dynasties perished was always women and petty men in groups, sunk in drink.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard the reason the Three Dynasties perished was always women and petty men in groups, sunk in drink.

sentences.zh: 《書》云：『乃用其婦人之言，四方之逋逃多罪，是信是使。』

translations.literal: The Documents says: "Then they used the words of their women, and those fleeing from the four quarters, many guilty, were trusted and employed."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says: "Then they used the words of their women, and those fleeing from the four quarters, many guilty, were trusted and employed."

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：『赫赫宗周、褒姒醤之。』

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."

sentences.zh: 『顛覆厥德，荒沈于酒。』

translations.literal: "They overturned their virtue and sank into drink."
translations.idiomatic: "They overturned their virtue and sank into drink."

sentences.zh: 及秦所以二世而亡者，養生大奢，奉終大厚。

translations.literal: As for Qin perishing in only two generations, it came from extreme luxury in life and excessive lavishness in funerary service.
translations.idiomatic: As for Qin perishing in only two generations, it came from extreme luxury in life and excessive lavishness in funerary service.

sentences.zh: 方今國家兼而有之，社稷宗廟之大憂也。」

translations.literal: At present the state combines both; this is the great worry of altars of soil and grain and ancestral temples."
translations.idiomatic: At present the state combines both; this is the great worry of altars of soil and grain and ancestral temples."

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君不任賢，厥妖天雨星。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler does not employ the worthy, its portent is stars raining from Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler does not employ the worthy, its portent is stars raining from Heaven."

sentences.zh: 文公十四年「七月，有星孛入于北斗」。

translations.literal: In Duke Wen year fourteen: "In seventh month, a broom star entered the Northern Dipper."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen year fourteen: "In seventh month, a broom star entered the Northern Dipper."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為孛者惡氣之所生也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that broom stars are generated by malignant qi.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that broom stars are generated by malignant qi.

sentences.zh: 謂之孛者，言其孛孛有所防蔽，闇亂不明之貌也。

translations.literal: It is called "bo" because its bristling aspect veils and obstructs, dark and disorderly, lacking clarity.
translations.idiomatic: It is called "bo" because its bristling aspect veils and obstructs, dark and disorderly, lacking clarity.

sentences.zh: 北斗，大國象。

translations.literal: The Northern Dipper is image of great states.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Dipper is image of great states.

sentences.zh: 後齊、宋、魯、莒、晉皆弒君。

translations.literal: Afterward Qi, Song, Lu, Ju, and Jin all had rulers killed by regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Qi, Song, Lu, Ju, and Jin all had rulers killed by regicide.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為君臣亂於朝，政令虧於外，則上濁三光之精，五星贏縮，變色逆行，甚則為孛。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that when ruler and ministers are chaotic at court and policies are deficient abroad, then above they foul the essences of the three lights, the five planets expand and contract, change color and move in reverse; in extreme cases this becomes a broom star.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that when ruler and ministers are chaotic at court and policies are deficient abroad, then above they foul the essences of the three lights, the five planets expand and contract, change color and move in reverse; in extreme cases this becomes a broom star.

sentences.zh: 北斗，人君象；

translations.literal: The Northern Dipper is image of the ruler;
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Dipper is image of the ruler;

sentences.zh: 孛星，亂臣類，篡殺之表也。

translations.literal: the broom star is of the type of disorderly ministers, a sign of usurpation and regicide.
translations.idiomatic: the broom star is of the type of disorderly ministers, a sign of usurpation and regicide.

sentences.zh: 星傳曰「魁者，貴人之牢」。

translations.literal: The Star Tradition says, "Kui is the prison of noble men."
translations.idiomatic: The Star Tradition says, "Kui is the prison of noble men."

sentences.zh: 又曰「孛星見北斗中，大臣諸侯有受誅者」。

translations.literal: It also says, "When a broom star appears in the Northern Dipper, there are great ministers or feudal lords who receive punishment."
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "When a broom star appears in the Northern Dipper, there are great ministers or feudal lords who receive punishment."

sentences.zh: 一曰魁為齊、晉。

translations.literal: Another view says Kui stands for Qi and Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says Kui stands for Qi and Jin.

sentences.zh: 夫彗星較然在北斗中，天之視人顯矣，史之有占明矣，時君終不改寤。

translations.literal: Now a conspicuous broom star within the Dipper shows Heaven's warning to men was clear, and historical divination was explicit, yet rulers of the time still did not reform.
translations.idiomatic: Now a conspicuous broom star within the Dipper shows Heaven's warning to men was clear, and historical divination was explicit, yet rulers of the time still did not reform.

sentences.zh: 是後，宋、魯、莒、晉、鄭、陳六國咸弒其君，齊再弒焉。

translations.literal: After this, six states - Song, Lu, Ju, Jin, Zheng, and Chen - all had rulers killed by regicide, and Qi suffered regicide twice.
translations.idiomatic: After this, six states - Song, Lu, Ju, Jin, Zheng, and Chen - all had rulers killed by regicide, and Qi suffered regicide twice.

sentences.zh: 中國既亂，夷狄並侵，兵革從橫，楚乘威席勝，深入諸夏，六侵伐，一滅國，觀兵周室。

translations.literal: Once the central states were in chaos, border peoples invaded together, warfare spread crosswise; Chu rode momentum and victory, drove deep into the Xia states, launched six invasions, destroyed one state, and reviewed troops before the Zhou royal house.
translations.idiomatic: Once the central states were in chaos, border peoples invaded together, warfare spread crosswise; Chu rode momentum and victory, drove deep into the Xia states, launched six invasions, destroyed one state, and reviewed troops before the Zhou royal house.

sentences.zh: 晉外滅二國，內敗王師，又連三國之兵大敗齊師于鞍，追亡逐北，東臨海水，威陵京師，武折大齊。

translations.literal: Jin destroyed two states abroad, defeated the royal army at home, then with forces of three states crushed Qi at An, pursued the fleeing to the sea in the east, overawed the capital, and broke mighty Qi's military power.
translations.idiomatic: Jin destroyed two states abroad, defeated the royal army at home, then with forces of three states crushed Qi at An, pursued the fleeing to the sea in the east, overawed the capital, and broke mighty Qi's military power.

sentences.zh: 皆孛星炎之所及，流至二十八年。

translations.literal: All this lay within the blaze reached by the broom star, extending to year twenty-eight.
translations.idiomatic: All this lay within the blaze reached by the broom star, extending to year twenty-eight.

sentences.zh: 星傳又曰：「彗星入北斗，有大戰。

translations.literal: The Star Tradition also says: "If a broom star enters the Northern Dipper, there will be great war.
translations.idiomatic: The Star Tradition also says: "If a broom star enters the Northern Dipper, there will be great war.

sentences.zh: 其流入北斗中，得名人；

translations.literal: If its tail enters the Dipper, named men are gained;
translations.idiomatic: If its tail enters the Dipper, named men are gained;

sentences.zh: 不入，失名人。」

translations.literal: if it does not enter, named men are lost."
translations.idiomatic: if it does not enter, named men are lost."

sentences.zh: 宋華元，賢名大夫，大棘之戰，華元獲於鄭，傳舉其效云。

translations.literal: Hua Yuan of Song, a worthy and renowned grandee: in the battle of Daji, Hua Yuan was captured by Zheng; the Tradition cites this as fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Hua Yuan of Song, a worthy and renowned grandee: in the battle of Daji, Hua Yuan was captured by Zheng; the Tradition cites this as fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰有星孛北斗，周史服曰：「不出七年，宋、齊、晉之君皆將死亂。」

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says there was a broom star in the Northern Dipper, and Zhou historian Fu said: "Within seven years, the rulers of Song, Qi, and Jin will all die in disorder."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says there was a broom star in the Northern Dipper, and Zhou historian Fu said: "Within seven years, the rulers of Song, Qi, and Jin will all die in disorder."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為北斗有環域，四星入其中也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that the Northern Dipper has a circled domain into which four stars entered.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that the Northern Dipper has a circled domain into which four stars entered.

sentences.zh: 斗，天之三辰，綱紀星也。

translations.literal: The Dipper is one of Heaven's three standards, the star of governing order.
translations.idiomatic: The Dipper is one of Heaven's three standards, the star of governing order.

sentences.zh: 宋、齊、晉，天子方伯，中國綱紀。

translations.literal: Song, Qi, and Jin were the Son of Heaven's regional lords, the governing framework of the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Song, Qi, and Jin were the Son of Heaven's regional lords, the governing framework of the central states.

sentences.zh: 彗所以除舊布新也。

translations.literal: A broom star serves to sweep out the old and distribute the new.
translations.idiomatic: A broom star serves to sweep out the old and distribute the new.

sentences.zh: 斗七星，故曰不出七年。

translations.literal: The Dipper has seven stars, hence "within seven years."
translations.idiomatic: The Dipper has seven stars, hence "within seven years."

sentences.zh: 至十六年，宋人弒昭公；

translations.literal: By year sixteen, men of Song regicide-killed Duke Zhao;
translations.idiomatic: By year sixteen, men of Song regicide-killed Duke Zhao;

sentences.zh: 十八年，齊人弒懿公；

translations.literal: in year eighteen, men of Qi regicide-killed Duke Yi;
translations.idiomatic: in year eighteen, men of Qi regicide-killed Duke Yi;

sentences.zh: 宣公二年，晉趙穿弒靈公。

translations.literal: and in Duke Xuan year two, Zhao Chuan of Jin regicide-killed Duke Ling.
translations.idiomatic: and in Duke Xuan year two, Zhao Chuan of Jin regicide-killed Duke Ling.

sentences.zh: 昭公十七年「冬，有星孛于大辰」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year seventeen: "In winter, a broom star appeared at Great Chen."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year seventeen: "In winter, a broom star appeared at Great Chen."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為大辰心也，心

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held Great Chen is Heart; Heart
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held Great Chen is Heart; Heart

sentences.zh: 哀公十三年「冬十一月，有星孛于東方」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ai year thirteen: "In winter, eleventh month, a broom star appeared in the east."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year thirteen: "In winter, eleventh month, a broom star appeared in the east."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為不言宿名者，不加宿也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that not naming a lodge means it was not fixed to one lodge.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that not naming a lodge means it was not fixed to one lodge.

sentences.zh: 以辰乘日而出，亂氣蔽君明也。

translations.literal: Emerging while riding the sun means disorderly qi veiling rulerly brightness.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging while riding the sun means disorderly qi veiling rulerly brightness.

sentences.zh: 明年，春秋事終。

translations.literal: The next year, Spring and Autumn affairs came to an end.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, Spring and Autumn affairs came to an end.

sentences.zh: 一曰，周之十一月，夏九月，日在氐。

translations.literal: Another view: Zhou eleventh month is Xia ninth month, with the sun in Di.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: Zhou eleventh month is Xia ninth month, with the sun in Di.

sentences.zh: 出東方者，軫、角、亢也。

translations.literal: Appearing in the east means Zhen, Jiao, and Kang.
translations.idiomatic: Appearing in the east means Zhen, Jiao, and Kang.

sentences.zh: 軫，楚；

translations.literal: Zhen is Chu;
translations.idiomatic: Zhen is Chu;

sentences.zh: 角、亢，陳、鄭也。

translations.literal: Jiao and Kang are Chen and Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao and Kang are Chen and Zheng.

sentences.zh: 或曰角、亢大國象，為齊、晉也。

translations.literal: Another says Jiao and Kang symbolize great states, namely Qi and Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Another says Jiao and Kang symbolize great states, namely Qi and Jin.

sentences.zh: 其後楚滅陳，田氏篡齊，六卿分晉，此其效也。

translations.literal: Afterward Chu destroyed Chen, the Tian clan usurped Qi, and the six ministers divided Jin; this was its fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Chu destroyed Chen, the Tian clan usurped Qi, and the six ministers divided Jin; this was its fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為孛，東方大辰也，不言大辰，旦而見與日爭光，星入而彗猶見。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held the broom star was Great Chen in the east. It did not say Great Chen because it appeared at dawn contending with the sun's light; even after the star entered, the broom-tail was still visible.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held the broom star was Great Chen in the east. It did not say Great Chen because it appeared at dawn contending with the sun's light; even after the star entered, the broom-tail was still visible.

sentences.zh: 是歲再失閏，十一月實八月也。

translations.literal: That year intercalation was missed twice, so eleventh month was actually eighth month.
translations.idiomatic: That year intercalation was missed twice, so eleventh month was actually eighth month.

sentences.zh: 日在鶉火，周分野也。

translations.literal: The sun was in Quail Fire, sector of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: The sun was in Quail Fire, sector of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 十四年冬，「有星孛」，在獲麟後。

translations.literal: In winter of year fourteen, "there was a broom star," after the capture of the qilin.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of year fourteen, "there was a broom star," after the capture of the qilin.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為不言所在，官失之也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that not stating its position was an official omission.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that not stating its position was an official omission.

sentences.zh: 高帝三年七月，有星孛于大角，旬餘乃入。

translations.literal: In year three under Emperor Gao, in seventh month, there was a broom star at Great Horn, entering after more than ten days.
translations.idiomatic: In year three under Emperor Gao, in seventh month, there was a broom star at Great Horn, entering after more than ten days.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時項羽為楚王，伯諸侯，而漢已定三秦，與羽相距滎陽，天下歸心於漢，楚將滅，故彗除王位也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that then Xiang Yu was king of Chu and hegemon over feudal lords, while Han had already pacified the three Qins and faced Xiang at Xingyang; hearts under heaven were turning to Han, Chu was about to perish, and so the broom swept away his kingship.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that then Xiang Yu was king of Chu and hegemon over feudal lords, while Han had already pacified the three Qins and faced Xiang at Xingyang; hearts under heaven were turning to Han, Chu was about to perish, and so the broom swept away his kingship.

sentences.zh: 一曰，項羽阬秦卒，燒宮室，弒義帝，亂王位，故彗加之也。

translations.literal: Another view: Xiang Yu buried alive Qin troops, burned palaces, and regicide-killed Emperor Yi, disrupting royal order, so the broom came upon him.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: Xiang Yu buried alive Qin troops, burned palaces, and regicide-killed Emperor Yi, disrupting royal order, so the broom came upon him.

sentences.zh: 文帝後七年九月，有星孛于西方，其本直尾、箕，末指虛、危，長丈餘，及天漢，十六日不見。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wen's later year seven, in ninth month, there was a broom star in the west, its root straight at Tail and Basket, its tip pointing to Void and Rooftop, over one zhang long and reaching the Milky River, disappearing after sixteen days.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen's later year seven, in ninth month, there was a broom star in the west, its root straight at Tail and Basket, its tip pointing to Void and Rooftop, over one zhang long and reaching the Milky River, disappearing after sixteen days.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為尾宋地，今楚彭城也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held Tail is Song territory, now Pengcheng of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held Tail is Song territory, now Pengcheng of Chu.

sentences.zh: 箕為燕，又為吳、越、齊。

translations.literal: Basket is Yan, and also Wu, Yue, and Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Basket is Yan, and also Wu, Yue, and Qi.

sentences.zh: 宿在漢中，負海之國水澤地也。

translations.literal: This lodge lay within Han and touched sea-facing states and marshy lands.
translations.idiomatic: This lodge lay within Han and touched sea-facing states and marshy lands.

sentences.zh: 是時景帝新立，信用晁錯，將誅正諸侯王，其象先見。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Jing was newly enthroned and trusted Chao Cuo, preparing to punish and rectify the feudal kings; the sign appeared first.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Emperor Jing was newly enthroned and trusted Chao Cuo, preparing to punish and rectify the feudal kings; the sign appeared first.

sentences.zh: 後三年，吳、楚、四齊與趙七國舉兵反，皆誅滅云。

translations.literal: Three years later, Wu, Chu, the four Qi states, and Zhao - seven states - raised troops in rebellion, and all were executed and destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, Wu, Chu, the four Qi states, and Zhao - seven states - raised troops in rebellion, and all were executed and destroyed.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元六年六月，有星孛于北方。

translations.literal: In Jianyuan year six under Emperor Wu, in sixth month, there was a broom star in the north.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianyuan year six under Emperor Wu, in sixth month, there was a broom star in the north.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為明年淮南王安入朝，與太尉武安侯田蚡有邪謀，而陳皇后驕恣，其後陳后廢，而淮南王反，誅。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that next year King An of Huainan came to court and had illicit plots with Grand Commandant Marquis Wu'an Tian Fen, while Empress Chen was arrogant and unrestrained; later Empress Chen was deposed and the King of Huainan rebelled and was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that next year King An of Huainan came to court and had illicit plots with Grand Commandant Marquis Wu'an Tian Fen, while Empress Chen was arrogant and unrestrained; later Empress Chen was deposed and the King of Huainan rebelled and was executed.

sentences.zh: 八月，長星出于東方，長終天，三十日去。

translations.literal: In eighth month, a long star emerged in the east, spanning to the end of heaven, and departed after thirty days.
translations.idiomatic: In eighth month, a long star emerged in the east, spanning to the end of heaven, and departed after thirty days.

sentences.zh: 占曰：「是為蚩尤旗，見則王者征伐四方。」

translations.literal: The divination says: "This is Chi You's banner; when seen, the king campaigns in all directions."
translations.idiomatic: The divination says: "This is Chi You's banner; when seen, the king campaigns in all directions."

sentences.zh: 其後兵誅四夷，連數十年。

translations.literal: Afterward troops punished the four border peoples for decades in succession.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward troops punished the four border peoples for decades in succession.

sentences.zh: 元狩四年四月，長星又出西北，是時伐胡尤甚。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou year four, in fourth month, a long star again emerged in the northwest; at this time campaigns against the Hu were especially intense.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou year four, in fourth month, a long star again emerged in the northwest; at this time campaigns against the Hu were especially intense.

sentences.zh: 宣帝地節元年正月，有星孛于西方，去太白二丈所。

translations.literal: In Dijie year one under Emperor Xuan, in first month, there was a broom star in the west, about two zhang from Venus.
translations.idiomatic: In Dijie year one under Emperor Xuan, in first month, there was a broom star in the west, about two zhang from Venus.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為太白為大將，彗孛加之，掃滅象也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held Venus is the great general; when broom-star falls upon it, it images sweeping annihilation.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held Venus is the great general; when broom-star falls upon it, it images sweeping annihilation.

sentences.zh: 明年，大將軍霍光薨，後二年家夷滅。

translations.literal: The next year Grand General Huo Guang died; two years later his household was exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Grand General Huo Guang died; two years later his household was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始元年正月，有星孛于營室，青白色，長六七丈，廣尺餘。

translations.literal: In Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, in first month, there was a broom star in Encampment, bluish-white, six or seven zhang long and over one chi wide.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, in first month, there was a broom star in Encampment, bluish-white, six or seven zhang long and over one chi wide.

sentences.zh: 劉向、谷永以為營室為後宮懷任之象，彗星加之，將有害懷任絕繼嗣者。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang and Gu Yong held Encampment symbolizes the empress's quarters and pregnancy; broom-star falling there meant there would be harm to pregnancy and succession cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang and Gu Yong held Encampment symbolizes the empress's quarters and pregnancy; broom-star falling there meant there would be harm to pregnancy and succession cut off.

sentences.zh: 一曰，後宮將受害也。

translations.literal: Another view: the harem itself would suffer harm.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: the harem itself would suffer harm.

sentences.zh: 其後許皇后坐祝詛後宮懷任者廢。

translations.literal: Afterward Empress Xu was deposed for cursing those in the harem who were pregnant.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Xu was deposed for cursing those in the harem who were pregnant.

sentences.zh: 趙皇后立妹為昭儀，害兩皇子，上遂無嗣。

translations.literal: Empress Zhao established her younger sister as Zhaoyi; she harmed two imperial sons, and the emperor ended without heir.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Zhao established her younger sister as Zhaoyi; she harmed two imperial sons, and the emperor ended without heir.

sentences.zh: 趙后姊妹卒皆伏辜。

translations.literal: In the end the Zhao sisters both met punishment.
translations.idiomatic: In the end the Zhao sisters both met punishment.

sentences.zh: 釐公十六年「正月戊申朔，隕石于宋，五，是月六鶂退飛過宋都」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year sixteen: "On wushen first day of first month, five stones fell in Song; in that same month six yi birds flew backward past the Song capital."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year sixteen: "On wushen first day of first month, five stones fell in Song; in that same month six yi birds flew backward past the Song capital."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為象宋襄公欲行伯道將自敗之戒也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held this warned Duke Xiang of Song, who wished to practice hegemony and would ruin himself.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held this warned Duke Xiang of Song, who wished to practice hegemony and would ruin himself.

sentences.zh: 石陰類，五陽數，自上而隕，此陰而陽行，欲高反下也。

translations.literal: Stone is a yin kind; five is a yang number. Falling from above is yin acting as yang, seeking height but reversing downward.
translations.idiomatic: Stone is a yin kind; five is a yang number. Falling from above is yin acting as yang, seeking height but reversing downward.

sentences.zh: 石與金同類，色以白為主，近白祥也。

translations.literal: Stone is of the same kind as metal and chiefly white in color, near a white portent.
translations.idiomatic: Stone is of the same kind as metal and chiefly white in color, near a white portent.

sentences.zh: 鶂水鳥，六陰數，退飛，欲進反退也。

translations.literal: Yi are water birds; six is a yin number. Flying backward means wishing to advance but instead retreating.
translations.idiomatic: Yi are water birds; six is a yin number. Flying backward means wishing to advance but instead retreating.

sentences.zh: 其色青，青祥也，屬於貌之不恭。

translations.literal: Their color is blue, a blue portent, belonging to irreverent bearing.
translations.idiomatic: Their color is blue, a blue portent, belonging to irreverent bearing.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，德薄國小，勿持炕陽，欲長諸侯，與彊大爭，必受其害。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: with thin virtue and small state, do not persist in overbearing yang and seek to dominate feudal lords by contending with the great and strong; you will certainly suffer harm.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: with thin virtue and small state, do not persist in overbearing yang and seek to dominate feudal lords by contending with the great and strong; you will certainly suffer harm.

sentences.zh: 襄公不寤，明年齊威死，伐齊喪，執滕子，圍曹，為盂之會，與楚爭盟，卒為所執。

translations.literal: Duke Xiang did not awaken. Next year Duke Wei of Qi died; Xiang attacked Qi during mourning, seized the ruler of Teng, besieged Cao, held the Meng assembly, contended with Chu for alliance, and was eventually captured by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang did not awaken. Next year Duke Wei of Qi died; Xiang attacked Qi during mourning, seized the ruler of Teng, besieged Cao, held the Meng assembly, contended with Chu for alliance, and was eventually captured by Chu.

sentences.zh: 後得反國，不悔過自責，復會諸侯伐鄭，與楚戰于泓，軍敗身傷，為諸侯笑。

translations.literal: After returning to his state he did not repent or blame himself, again assembled feudal lords to attack Zheng, fought Chu at Hong, was defeated and wounded, and became the laughingstock of feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: After returning to his state he did not repent or blame himself, again assembled feudal lords to attack Zheng, fought Chu at Hong, was defeated and wounded, and became the laughingstock of feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰：隕石，星也；

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: fallen stones are stars;
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: fallen stones are stars;

sentences.zh: 鶂退飛，風也。

translations.literal: and yi flying backward is wind.
translations.idiomatic: and yi flying backward is wind.

sentences.zh: 宋襄公以問周內史叔興曰：「是何祥也？

translations.literal: Duke Xiang asked Zhou internal historian Shu Xing: "What omen is this?
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang asked Zhou internal historian Shu Xing: "What omen is this?

sentences.zh: 吉凶何在？」

translations.literal: Where do fortune and misfortune lie?"
translations.idiomatic: Where do fortune and misfortune lie?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「今茲魯多大喪，明年齊有亂，君將得諸侯而不終。」

translations.literal: He replied: "This year Lu has many great deaths; next year Qi will have disorder. You will gain the feudal lords but not complete your end."
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "This year Lu has many great deaths; next year Qi will have disorder. You will gain the feudal lords but not complete your end."

sentences.zh: 退而告人曰：「是陰陽之事，非吉凶之所生也。

translations.literal: After withdrawing he told others: "These are matters of yin and yang, not what gives rise to fortune and misfortune.
translations.idiomatic: After withdrawing he told others: "These are matters of yin and yang, not what gives rise to fortune and misfortune.

sentences.zh: 吉凶繇人，吾不敢逆君故也。」

translations.literal: Fortune and misfortune come from men; I dared not oppose my ruler, therefore I spoke so."
translations.idiomatic: Fortune and misfortune come from men; I dared not oppose my ruler, therefore I spoke so."

sentences.zh: 是歲，魯公子季友、鄫季姬、公孫茲皆卒。

translations.literal: That year Prince Jiyou of Lu, Ji Ji of Zeng, and Gongsun Zi all died.
translations.idiomatic: That year Prince Jiyou of Lu, Ji Ji of Zeng, and Gongsun Zi all died.

sentences.zh: 明年齊威死，適庶亂。

translations.literal: The next year Duke Wei of Qi died, and disorder broke out between legitimate and secondary heirs.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Duke Wei of Qi died, and disorder broke out between legitimate and secondary heirs.

sentences.zh: 宋襄公伐齊行伯，卒為楚所敗。

translations.literal: Duke Xiang of Song attacked Qi to practice hegemony, but was ultimately defeated by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang of Song attacked Qi to practice hegemony, but was ultimately defeated by Chu.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為是歲歲在壽星，其衝降婁。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that that year Jupiter was in Shouxing, with its opposing position in Jianglou.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that that year Jupiter was in Shouxing, with its opposing position in Jianglou.

sentences.zh: 降婁，魯分野也，故為魯多大喪。

translations.literal: Jianglou is Lu's sector, hence Lu suffered many great deaths.
translations.idiomatic: Jianglou is Lu's sector, hence Lu suffered many great deaths.

sentences.zh: 正月，日在星紀，厭在玄枵。

translations.literal: In first month, the sun was in Xingji, with opposition in Xuanxiao.
translations.idiomatic: In first month, the sun was in Xingji, with opposition in Xuanxiao.

sentences.zh: 玄枵，齊分野也。

translations.literal: Xuanxiao is Qi's sector.
translations.idiomatic: Xuanxiao is Qi's sector.

sentences.zh: 石，山物；

translations.literal: Stone is a mountain thing;
translations.idiomatic: Stone is a mountain thing;

sentences.zh: 齊，大嶽後。

translations.literal: and Qi descends from the line after the Great Peak.
translations.idiomatic: and Qi descends from the line after the Great Peak.

sentences.zh: 五石象齊威卒而五公子作亂，故為明年齊有亂。

translations.literal: Five stones symbolized Duke Wei of Qi dying and five princes causing disorder, hence "next year Qi will have disorder."
translations.idiomatic: Five stones symbolized Duke Wei of Qi dying and five princes causing disorder, hence "next year Qi will have disorder."

sentences.zh: 庶民惟星，隕於宋，象宋襄將得諸侯之眾，而治五公子之亂。

translations.literal: "The common people are stars"; stars fell in Song, symbolizing that Duke Xiang would obtain the feudal lords' following and quell the disorder of the five princes.
translations.idiomatic: "The common people are stars"; stars fell in Song, symbolizing that Duke Xiang would obtain the feudal lords' following and quell the disorder of the five princes.

sentences.zh: 星隕而鶂退飛，故為得諸侯而不終。

translations.literal: Since stars fell and yi birds flew backward, it signified obtaining the feudal lords yet not completing his end.
translations.idiomatic: Since stars fell and yi birds flew backward, it signified obtaining the feudal lords yet not completing his end.

sentences.zh: 六鶂象後六年伯業始退，執於盂也。

translations.literal: The six yi birds symbolized that six years later his hegemonic enterprise would begin to decline, and he would be captured at Meng.
translations.idiomatic: The six yi birds symbolized that six years later his hegemonic enterprise would begin to decline, and he would be captured at Meng.

sentences.zh: 民反德為亂，亂則妖災生，言吉凶繇人，然后陰陽衝厭受其咎。

translations.literal: When the people turn against virtue and produce disorder, then portents and disasters arise; fortune and misfortune come from men, and then yin-yang opposition and affliction impose their blame.
translations.idiomatic: When the people turn against virtue and produce disorder, then portents and disasters arise; fortune and misfortune come from men, and then yin-yang opposition and affliction impose their blame.

sentences.zh: 齊、魯之災非君所致，故曰「吾不敢逆君故也」。

translations.literal: The calamities of Qi and Lu were not caused by the ruler of Song, hence he said, "I did not dare oppose my ruler, therefore I spoke so."
translations.idiomatic: The calamities of Qi and Lu were not caused by the ruler of Song, hence he said, "I did not dare oppose my ruler, therefore I spoke so."

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「距諫自彊，茲謂卻行，厥異鶂退飛。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Rejecting remonstrance and strengthening oneself is called retrograde conduct; its anomaly is yi birds flying backward.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Rejecting remonstrance and strengthening oneself is called retrograde conduct; its anomaly is yi birds flying backward.

sentences.zh: 適當黜，則鶂退飛。」

translations.literal: When the legitimate should be demoted, yi birds fly backward."
translations.idiomatic: When the legitimate should be demoted, yi birds fly backward."

sentences.zh: 惠帝三年，隕石綿諸，一。

translations.literal: In Emperor Hui year three, one meteorite fell at Mianzhu.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Hui year three, one meteorite fell at Mianzhu.

sentences.zh: 武帝征和四年二月丁酉，隕石雍，二，天晏亡雲，聲聞四百里。

translations.literal: On dingyou day of second month, Zhenghe year four under Emperor Wu, two meteorites fell in Yong; the sky was calm and cloudless, and the sound was heard for four hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou day of second month, Zhenghe year four under Emperor Wu, two meteorites fell in Yong; the sky was calm and cloudless, and the sound was heard for four hundred li.

sentences.zh: 元帝建昭元年正月戊辰，隕石梁國，六。

translations.literal: On wuchen day of first month, Jianzhao year one under Emperor Yuan, six meteorites fell in Liang.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen day of first month, Jianzhao year one under Emperor Yuan, six meteorites fell in Liang.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始四年正月癸卯，隕石槁，四，肥累，一。

translations.literal: On guimao day of first month, Jianshi year four under Emperor Cheng, four meteorites fell at Gao and one at Feilei.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao day of first month, Jianshi year four under Emperor Cheng, four meteorites fell at Gao and one at Feilei.

sentences.zh: 陽朔三年二月壬戌，隕石白馬，八。

translations.literal: On renxu day of second month, Yangshuo year three, eight meteorites fell at Baima.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu day of second month, Yangshuo year three, eight meteorites fell at Baima.

sentences.zh: 鴻嘉二年五月癸未，隕石杜衍，三。

translations.literal: On guiwei day of fifth month, Hongjia year two, three meteorites fell at Duyan.
translations.idiomatic: On guiwei day of fifth month, Hongjia year two, three meteorites fell at Duyan.

sentences.zh: 元延四年三月，隕石都關，二。

translations.literal: In third month of Yuanyan year four, two meteorites fell at Duguan.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Yuanyan year four, two meteorites fell at Duguan.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平元年正月丁未，隕石北地，十。

translations.literal: On dingwei day of first month, Jianping year one under Emperor Ai, ten meteorites fell in Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei day of first month, Jianping year one under Emperor Ai, ten meteorites fell in Beidi.

sentences.zh: 其九月甲辰，隕石虞，二。

translations.literal: That ninth month, on jiachen day, two meteorites fell at Yu.
translations.idiomatic: That ninth month, on jiachen day, two meteorites fell at Yu.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始二年六月，隕石鉅鹿，二。

translations.literal: In sixth month of Yuanshi year two under Emperor Ping, two meteorites fell in Julu.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of Yuanshi year two under Emperor Ping, two meteorites fell in Julu.

sentences.zh: 自惠盡平，隕石凡十一，皆有光燿雷聲，成、哀尤屢。

translations.literal: From Emperor Hui through Emperor Ping there were eleven meteorite-fall events in total, all with flashes and thunderous sound, and especially frequent under Emperors Cheng and Ai.
translations.idiomatic: From Emperor Hui through Emperor Ping there were eleven meteorite-fall events in total, all with flashes and thunderous sound, and especially frequent under Emperors Cheng and Ai.
